# n8n 2.0 ist jetzt in unserem 1-Klick-Image verfügbar | Contabo Blog

[Zum Inhalt springen](https://contabo.com/blog/de/n8n-2-0-ist-jetzt-in-unserem-1-klick-image-verfuegbar/#content "Zum Inhalt springen")

Hosten Sie Ihren eigenen KI-Agenten mit [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/de/openclaw-hosting/)
 - kostenlose 1-Klick-Installation!

![n8n 2.0 ist jetzt in unserem 1-Klick-Image verfügbar (Titelbild)](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/blog-head_-n8n-2_DE.webp)

n8n hat gerade die Version 2.0 veröffentlicht. Wenn du ein neues Interface und flashy Features erwartest: Dieses Release ist nicht dafür gedacht. Aber die Verbesserungen sind definitiv einen Blick wert.

Version 2.0 bringt vor allem Security-Hardening, Zuverlässigkeits-Fixes und Performance-Verbesserungen – dafür waren jedoch nicht abwärtskompatible Änderungen nötig. Genau die Art von Änderungen, die n8n seit über zwei Jahren veröffentlichen wollte, aber ohne ein großes Update nicht umsetzen konnte. Jetzt sind sie hier, und deine selbst gehostete n8n-Instanz auf Contabo wird sicherer und zuverlässiger, ohne dass du viel tun musst.

Was sich tatsächlich in n8n 2.0 geändert hat
--------------------------------------------

**Task Runner sind jetzt standardmäßig aktiviert**. Das bedeutet, dass alle Ausführungen von Code-Knoten in isolierten Umgebungen mit eingeschränktem Zugriff auf dein System erfolgen. Früher konnten Code-Knoten auf Umgebungsvariablen zugreifen und hatten breitere Systemberechtigungen. Jetzt tun sie das nicht – es sei denn, du aktivierst dieses Verhalten ausdrücklich.

**Sicherheit wird überall verschärft.** Der Zugriff auf Umgebungsvariablen von Code-Knoten ist standardmäßig blockiert. Knoten, die die Ausführung willkürlicher Befehle erlauben (ExecuteCommand und LocalFileTrigger), sind standardmäßig deaktiviert. OAuth-Callbacks erfordern eine Authentifizierung. Dateioperationen sind auf bestimmte Verzeichnisse beschränkt, es sei denn, es wird anders eingestellt.

Diese Änderungen machen n8n out of the box deutlich sicherer und das ist wichtig, wenn du Workflows betreibst, die Kundendaten, API-Zugangsdaten oder interne Systeme verarbeiten.

**Die Unterstützung für Datenbanken wurde bereinigt.** MySQL- und MariaDB-Support sind vollständig weggefallen. PostgreSQL ist jetzt die empfohlene Produktionsdatenbank, wobei SQLite eine Option für kleinere Deployments bleibt. Der neue SQLite-Pooling-Treiber kann in Benchmarks bis zu 10× schneller sein. Wenn du also SQLite nutzt, wirst du automatisch Performance-Verbesserungen sehen.

**Das Verhalten von Sub-Workflows hat sich geändert.** Früher hat ein Parent-Workflow falsche Ergebnisse erhalten, wenn er einen Sub-Workflow aufgerufen hat, der Wait-Nodes oder Webhooks enthielt. Jetzt erhält er korrekt die Ausgabe vom Ende des Child-Workflows. Wenn du Sub-Workflows mit Human-in-the-Loop-Nodes nutzt, ist dieser Fix wichtig.

**Die Handhabung von Binärdaten hat sich geändert.** Der alte In-Memory-Modus für Binärdaten ist weg. Binärdaten werden jetzt je nach Ihrer Konfiguration im Dateisystem oder in der Datenbank gespeichert. Das macht das System unter Last stabiler, du brauchst dafür aber ausreichend freien Speicherplatz.

Die vollständige Liste der Breaking Changes findest du in der offiziellen [n8n-Dokumentation](https://docs.n8n.io/2-0-breaking-changes/)
. Dort wird jede Änderung erklärt – inklusive Tipps, wie du damit umgehst.

Ein neues Feature: Publish vs. Save
-----------------------------------

In n8n 1.x wurde beim Speichern eines aktivierten Workflows sofort die produktive Version überschrieben. Das kann unpraktisch sein, wenn du Änderungen an einem aktiven Workflow testest.

Version 2.0 trennt diese Aktionen. **Save** bewahrt deine Änderungen, ohne das Live-System zu ändern. **Publish** ist jetzt eine separate, explizite Aktion, die die Produktionsversion aktualisiert, wenn alles bereit ist. Klingt nach einer kleinen Änderung, reduziert aber deutlich das Risiko, dass du beim Bearbeiten aus Versehen produktive Workflows kaputt machst.

Dies legt auch den Grundstein für die automatische Speicherung, die im Januar 2026 kommt.

Aktualisieren einer vorhandenen n8n-Instanz
-------------------------------------------

Wenn du n8n vor Version 2.0 über unser 1-Klick-Image auf Contabo installiert hast, läuft bei dir Version 1.x. Das musst du wissen:

**Version 1.x erhält noch für drei Monate Support.** Nur Fixes für Bugs und Sicherheit, keine neuen Funktionen. Spätestens danach solltest du auf der 2.0 sein.

**Überprüfe zuerst den Migration Report.** n8n hat ein Tool gebaut, das deine Instanz scannt und dir genau sagt, welche Workflows und Konfigurationen vor dem Upgrade angepasst werden müssen. Du findest es in deiner n8n-Instanz unter **Settings→ Migration Report** (du brauchst globalen Administratorzugang, um es zu sehen).

Der Report unterscheidet zwei Kategorien: Probleme auf Workflow-Ebene (bestimmte Knoten oder Verhaltensweisen, die brechen) und Probleme auf Instanz-Ebene (Umgebungsvariablen und Serverkonfiguration). Jedes Problem wird nach Schweregrad kategorisiert. Kümmere dich zuerst um die kritischen Punkte – sie würden Workflows brechen. Mittel- und niedrigpriorisierte Punkte können warten, sollten aber auch adressiert werden.

Wenn du alles abgearbeitet hast und den Report aktualisierst, heißt ein sauberer Report: Du kannst upgraden.

**Migrationspfade sind pro Issue dokumentiert.** Für die meisten Breaking Changes stellt n8n spezifische Anweisungen bereit, was angepasst werden muss. Einige erfordern Änderungen an Umgebungsvariablen, andere benötigen Anpassungen an Workflows. Das Migrationstool verlinkt auf detaillierte Dokumentationen für jedes Problem.

**Die Community-Edition erhält alle Änderungen.** Alle Änderungen in 2.0 gelten für selbst gehostete Community-Editionen, Cloud-Instanzen und Enterprise-Versionen. Wenn du selbst hostest, fehlt dir nichts.

Neue Installationen erhalten 2.0 automatisch
--------------------------------------------

Ab sofort bekommst du bei einem neuen Server mit kostenlosem n8n-Setup über unser 1-Klick-Image standardmäßig n8n 2.0 (stable). Keine Migration nötig – nur die neuen Security-Defaults und das verbesserte Verhalten von Anfang an.

Das ist praktisch, wenn du ein neues Projekt startest oder 2.0 erstmal in einer separaten Umgebung testen willst, bevor du deine produktive Instanz migrierst.

Vorhandene Instanz upgraden
---------------------------

Wenn du n8n schon auf deinem Contabo VPS oder VDS betreibst und deinen Server nicht neu installieren willst, kannst du deine n8n-Instanz natürlich auch selbst aktualisieren. Unser Leitfaden zu [So aktualisierst du deine selbstgehostete n8n-Instanz auf Contabo](https://contabo.com/blog/de/so-aktualisierst-du-deine-selbstgehostete-n8n-instanz-auf-contabo/)
 führt dich durch alle notwendigen Schritte.

Wie geht’s weiter mit n8n?
--------------------------

n8n will nicht noch einmal zwei Jahre bis Version 3.0 warten. Künftig sind ein bis zwei große Releases pro Jahr geplant, damit Breaking Changes schneller ausgeliefert werden, statt sich zu einem einzigen riesigen Update anzustauen.

Kurzfristig kommt im Januar 2026 Autosave. Danach stehen weitere Verbesserungen am Canvas sowie neue Workflow-Management-Features auf der Roadmap.

Lohnt sich das Upgrade?
-----------------------

Wenn du produktive Workflows auf n8n 1.x laufen hast: Ja, aber nicht sofort. Führe zuerst den Migration Report aus. Behebe kritische Probleme. Teste das Upgrade in einer Nicht-Produktivumgebung, falls du eine hast. Und dann: upgraden.

Wenn du neu startest oder nur leichte Workloads hast, ist Version 2.0 (stable) die richtige Version für dein Setup.

Für Teams mit sensiblen Workflows oder Compliance-Anforderungen rechtfertigen allein die Security-Verbesserungen das Upgrade. Isolierte Task Runner, blockierter Zugriff auf Umgebungsvariablen und standardmäßig deaktivierte Knoten für beliebige Command-Ausführung reduzieren die Angriffsfläche deutlich.

Unser kostenloses 1-Klick-n8n-Image macht die Bereitstellung einfach – egal ob du neu installierst oder eine bestehende Instanz betreibst. Das Image übernimmt Installation, Konfiguration und das initiale Setup, damit du dich auf deine Workflows konzentrieren kannst statt auf Server-Setup.

Version 2.0 sorgt nicht für Schlagzeilen mit flashy Features, aber sie macht n8n sicherer und zuverlässiger für den Produktivbetrieb. Und das ist wichtiger als ein neues Interface oder neue Node-Typen, wenn deine Workflows wirklich relevant für dein Business sind.

# Does My Server Come With DDoS Protection?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000269957-does-my-server-come-with-ddos-protection/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Does my server come with DDoS protection?  
**

**All Contabo Servers and Webspace packages are equipped with a DDoS protection by default.** 

You don’t have to do anything in order to start the DDoS protection – the Contabo **DDoS protection is activated automatically and free of charge for all Contabo servers.**

We are constantly working to improve the Contabo protection system so it can repel many different types of attacks. 

However, just like any other DDoS protection, the Contabo protection system cannot provide a guarantee that every conceivable DDoS attack will be repelled.

#### **How does Contabo DDoS work?**

The Contabo DDoS protection automatically detects most DDoS attack patterns and which filters the incoming traffic to the server so that the “malicious” attacking traffic is dropped and only the “real” desired traffic arrives at your server. 

This means that you will barely notice a possible attack while our DDoS protection filters the ongoing attack for you.

You can read more about DDoS at Contabo on the [Contabo website here](https://contabo.com/en/ddos-protection/)
.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000269957-does-my-server-come-with-ddos-protection-)

If you’ve ordered a new service and would like to cancel it immediately, you can contact our support team who will be able to help you process the cancellation and refund of your service.

# How do I Receive My Server Details After Buying a Contabo Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000253457-how-do-i-receive-my-server-details-after-buying-a-contabo-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What happens after I buy a Contabo server?**

Once you have placed your order and your payment has been received, your instance will be automatically provisioned. 

If this is your first order a new Contabo account will be created for you automatically, as well as the credentials necessary to log in to the [Customer Control Panel](https://new.contabo.com/)
 for the first time.

#### **How long does it take to get access to my server?**

The delivery time can vary depending on the server you have ordered, but if you are still waiting for your login details you can check the **[status of your order here](https://contabo.com/en/order-status/)
**. When it’s ready, you’ll receive a login email from Contabo with the subject “_Your login data!_”.

The “_Your login data!_” data email will be sent to the email address you provided during the ordering process so make sure you check the correct inbox for this email. Also check your spam folder for this email to make sure you don’t miss it.

#### **What is included in the login data email?**

If this is your first order, the “_Your login data!_” email will contain your Customer ID and a login and password for the [Customer Control Panel](https://new.contabo.com/)
. 

As well as login details for the [Customer Control Panel](https://new.contabo.com/)
 and your server, the email may also contain various information specific to your order. Be sure to save the information in this email, as it contains important credentials, such as:

*   IP addresses
*   Server usernames
*   Logins for additional services. e.g web management panels, FTP Space, Full-Monitoring, Firewalls or Additional IPs
*   VNC login credentials

**Please note****,** it is recommended that you save all the information from the “_Your login data!_” email to a password manager for safekeeping. Once you have saved your information, delete this email as saving login details in an email can be a security risk.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000253457-how-do-i-receive-my-server-details-after-buying-a-contabo-server-)

# How to implement a CDN  | Contabo Blog

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-implement-cdn/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

Speed, stability, and global reach are considered a necessity for any modern website. One of the most effective ways to achieve all three is to use a content delivery network (CDN). In this guide, we explain what a CDN is, how it works, and the exact steps you need to take to set it up for your Contabo-hosted website. 

What is a CDN 
--------------

A content delivery network (CDN) is a globally distributed network of servers that stores (or caches) your website’s static content, such as images, CSS, and JavaScript files. When visitors access your website, the CDN delivers these files from the nearest server location, resulting in faster load times and smoother performance. 

Using a CDN offers several benefits: 

*   Faster page loads 

*   Lower server load 

*   Extra DDoS protection 

*   Better global performance 

By distributing your content across multiple servers worldwide, a CDN reduces the load on your main server and ensures consistent performance for users, regardless of their location. 

Setting Up CDN 
---------------

For this tutorial, we are using [Cloudflare](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/)
 as an example because it integrates seamlessly with Contabo servers and offers a free plan that covers all basic features. In addition to Cloudflare, you can also use the [vshosting CDN](https://contabo.com/en/cdn-vshosting/)
 provided by Contabo. 

### Step 1: Choosing a CDN Partner 

The first step is to select a CDN provider that meets your particular needs. Popular and reliable options include [Cloudflare](https://www.cloudflare.com/)
, [Bunny.net](https://bunny.net/)
, [Amazon CloudFront](https://aws.amazon.com/de/cloudfront/)
, and [KeyCDN](https://www.keycdn.com/)
. 

1.  Visit [Cloudflare.com](https://www.cloudflare.com/)
     

2.  Sign up for an account 

3.  Click “**Add Site**” and enter your domain name 

Cloudflare automatically scans your DNS records and prepares your website for setup. 

### Step 2: Update Your DNS Settings 

Once Cloudflare has scanned your entries, you will receive new nameservers. You’ll need to update these with your **domain registrar**: 

1.  Log into your domain registrar dashboard. 

2.  Find your current DNS or nameserver settings. 

3.  Replace the existing nameservers with the ones provided by Cloudflare. 

DNS propagation can take up to **24 hours**, though it often completes sooner. 

Once this process is done, your domain will be active on Cloudflare’s CDN. 

### Step 3: Configure Your CDN 

Once your domain is connected, optimize your CDN settings for best performance. To do this, enable the following features in your Cloudflare dashboard: 

*   **Caching:** Stores your website’s static assets for quicker delivery. 

*   **Minification:** Compresses your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files to reduce load time. 

*   **Compression:** Turn on **Brotli** or **Gzip** compression to reduce file sizes further. 

*   [**SSL**](https://contabo.com/blog/ultimate-guide-to-ssl/)
     **(HTTPS):** Secure your website traffic with encrypted connections. 

These optimizations can significantly boost your site’s loading speed and improve its overall stability. 

### Step 4: Test Your Setup 

Once your CDN is live and configured, it’s time to test your website’s performance. You can use free tools such as [**GTmetrix**](https://gtmetrix.com/)
 or [**Google PageSpeed Insights**](https://developers.google.com/speed/pagespeed/insights_extensions?hl=de)
 to measure the improvements. 

You should notice: 

*   Faster loading times 

*   Lower resource usage 

*   Improved performance scores 

Using a CDN with WordPress 
---------------------------

If your website runs on **WordPress**, connecting your CDN is even easier. 

1.  Install the **Cloudflare plugin** directly from your WordPress dashboard. 

2.  Log into your Cloudflare account through the plugin. 

3.  Enable caching and other optimization features automatically. 

If you make changes to the design or content, clear your CDN cache to ensure that visitors only see the latest version. In the Cloudflare plugin, this is done by selecting “**Purge Cache**”. 

Watch Our YouTube Video on CDN 
-------------------------------

If you are more of a visual learner, we have a Video on our YouTube ready for you, where we walk you through the whole process. 

Conclusion 
-----------

And that’s it! You have successfully set up a CDN to make your website faster, more secure, and more efficient. Whether you host on a [Contabo VPS](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
 or a [dedicated server](https://contabo.com/en/dedicated-servers/)
, integrating a CDN is a helpful step that ensures better performance and global availability.

# What is the Nextcloud Tool?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305621-what-is-the-nextcloud-tool/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is the Nextcloud tool?**

[Nextcloud](https://nextcloud.com/)
 can be used to provide file hosting services with browser based access but also desktop and mobile applications. It can easily be connected to Contabo’s Object Storage in order to store and retrieve data.

Please follow the installation instruction [here](https://nextcloud.com/)
 if you are interested in using this tool. You will need the following information if you want to install and use Nextcloud with Contabo’s Object Storage:

*   access\_key
*   secret\_key
*   Contabo’s S3 URL
*   your bucket name

#### **How do I setup Nextcloud with Contabo’s Object Storage?**

Please follow the steps below in order to use Contabo’s Object Storage as external storage:

1.  Select on the upper right admin user and then ” Apps”.
    
    ![Nextcloud Add App](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133982598/original/T3m6pwmRx3T9RAXFmVSyYNTucTk4gxQwAg.png?1722326603)
    
2.  Enable “External storage support”.
    
    ![Nextcloud Add App](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133982599/original/xMNImOc2rj0oXBBMzvD7ZU0DuI3vG0FsOg.png?1722326603)
    
3.  Go to “Settings” via upper right admin user.  
    ![Nextcloud Add App](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133986355/original/ZOQbesIts2yuBBVt7mFz8Ogymm462-iWTQ.png?1722327004)
4.  On left hand side, via the Administration section, select “External Storage”.
    
    ![Nextcloud Add App](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133982600/original/veQN4GiVXLvhheLSNqaEbhRN-OsDFsW3fw.png?1722326603)
    
5.  Specify the values for a new external storage as outlined below:
    
    Folder name e.g. Contabo S3 Object Storage  
    External Storage = e.g Amazon S3  
    Authentication = Access Key  
    Bucket = e.g nextcloud  
    Hostname = e.g eu2.contabostorage.com  
    Port = e.g 443  
    Region = default  
    Check Enable SSL  
    Check Enable Path Style  
    Access Key = e.g 82046e8110804a43bf29c1ae426a724d  
    Secret Key = e.g 82e69bd7a52076c527154297a76c2233
    
    ![Nextcloud Add App](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133982601/original/CG9IuB2bjDLcCu_-7Hs2PJ5z8ngrShojyQ.png?1722326603)
    
6.  Click save (the little checkmark icon at the right).
7.  Navigate to files, there you should see the folder “Contabo S3 Object Storage” and you are now able to upload and download files to the S3 Object Storage.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000305621-what-is-the-nextcloud-tool-)

# Can I Use My Own Windows License on My Contabo Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000270398-can-i-use-my-own-windows-license-on-my-contabo-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Can I Use My Own Windows License on My Contabo Server?**

If you have your own windows license, it can only be used on a Dedicated Server. **It is not possible to use your own Windows license on a Virtual Private Server (VPS) or a Virtual Dedicated Server (VDS).**

Due to the license agreement between Contabo and Microsoft, it is not possible to use your own Windows license on a VPS or VDS. **Instead, you must use the Windows Server license provided by Contabo.** The terms of the license agreement require Contabo to charge a monthly fee for a Windows Server, which is determined by the size of the VPS/VDS you order.

**If you have a Contabo dedicated server, you can use your own Windows license on it.**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270398-can-i-use-my-own-windows-license-on-my-contabo-server-)

# How Do I Rollback My Server to a Snapshot?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000270389-how-do-i-rollback-my-server-to-a-snapshot/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is the rollback function?  
**

The rollback feature allows you to restore your Virtual Private Server (VPS) to the state it was in at the time of a snapshot. This feature allows you to take a snapshot of your VPS when it is working properly. Then, if you perform an upgrade that causes problems for your VPS, you can simply roll back the VPS to the state it was in before the upgrade.

#### **How do I rollback my server to a previous snapshot?**

1.  Log in to the [Customer Support Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click “VPS control” from the blue “control panel” section on the left hand side of the page.
3.  Click the button in the Snapshot column next to the VPS to which you want to rollback.
4.  Press the “Rollback” button next to the active snapshot you would like to rollback.
5.  Once done you will get a notification that says “The snapshot has been rolledback successfully!”.

Once the rollback is complete, you can use your VPS in the state it was in when the snapshot was taken.

Note that **when you perform a rollback of your snapshot, the system will automatically delete any newer snapshots during the rollback.** If this is the case, you will receive a warning that more recent snapshots will be deleted. 

Also, **after a rollback, the VPS will be restored to the specifications of the VPS at the time the snapshot was taken.** So if you have upgraded your VPS since the snapshot was taken, the rollback will also remove any upgrades you have made to the specifications of your VPS.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270389-how-do-i-rollback-my-server-to-a-snapshot-)

# Why am I Seeing a Black Screen When I'm Connected to My Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000270402-why-am-i-seeing-a-black-screen-when-i-m-connected-to-my-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Why am I seeing a black screen when I’m connected to my server?**

If you see a black screen after the initial connection when connecting via VNC, then this is correct, just **click into the VNC window and press the Enter key. Your operating system will then respond with the login screen.**

  

If this doesn’t work we also recommend rebooting your server. You can find a guide on how to [reboot your server here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270047--draft-do-not-use-how-can-i-restart-my-server-/preview)
.

  

#### **What should I do if I’m still having issues with my server?**

If you’re still experiencing problems, you can contact Customer Support.

  

Please provide the customer support team with as much of the following information as possible to help expedite their investigation:

  

*   When did the problem first occur?
*   What activities were in progress when the problem occurred? 
*   Is the problem persistent or temporary? 
*   Can you provide log files of the problem?
*   Can you provide any error messages or error codes you received?
*   What programs or applications are installed on the server that may be interfering with the setup?
*   Please provide your login and port information so that the support team can access your server for diagnosis.  
    

You can raise a ticket with the customer support team via the [](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/en/support/home)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
.

# What is the Customer Control Panel and How do I Access it?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000268754-what-is-the-customer-control-panel-and-how-do-i-access-it/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is the Customer Control Panel?**

The [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 is your interface for interacting with your Contabo products, ordering new products and managing your Contabo account and billing details.

#### **How do I access the Customer Control Panel?**

To access the Customer Control Panel go to **[](https://my.contabo.com/)
**[](https://my.contabo.com/)
[https://my.contabo.com](https://my.contabo.com/)
[](https://my.contabo.com/)
**[](https://my.contabo.com/)
** and log in with the login details sent to you in the “_Your login data!_” email when you first ordered a Contabo product. 

**For security reasons,** we advise you to change your password immediately after your first login, which you can do on the [Customer details page](https://my.contabo.com/account/details)
. To better protect your account, don’t forget to set-up 2-factor authentication on the same page. You can follow our guide on how to setup 2-factor authentication **[here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000305452-how-do-i-change-my-2-factor-authentication-settings-)
**.

Once you have setup your basic credentials on the [Customer details page](https://my.contabo.com/account/details)
, take your time to get familiar with the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
.

**Please note** that the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/account/login)
 is only available to users with active subscriptions.

#### **How do I control my server with the Customer Control Panel?**

You can control your server by using [VDS control](https://my.contabo.com/vds)
, [VPS control](https://my.contabo.com/vps#)
 or [Server control](https://my.contabo.com/dservers)
 depending on the product you have purchased. These pages allow you to perform key actions to control your server, such as starting or stopping your servers, starting a rescue system or creating a snapshot (VPS only).

**![Basic control panel for your instance](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103079568745/original/zVXB22EJEmWOzHANDPbwdfiIH9shjHx2qQ.png?1705316263)**

#### **How do I find my billing and payment details on the Customer Control Panel?**

Billing and payment details on the [Billing page here](https://my.contabo.com/account/payment)
. The [billing page](https://my.contabo.com/account/payment)
 allows you to view your payment history, current balance and your invoices. 

You can also access the [Payment Method page here](https://my.contabo.com/account/payment_method)
 to manage your payment method or top up your balance.

#### **I can’t login to the Customer Contol Panel, what do I do?**

There are a number of things that can prevent you from logging into the **[Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
**. **The best thing to do is read our article** [**here**](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280033-why-can-t-i-log-in-to-the-customer-control-panel-)
 [](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280033-why-can-t-i-log-in-to-the-customer-control-panel-)
which provides more information on what to do if you can’t log in to the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
.[](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280033-why-can-t-i-log-in-to-the-customer-control-panel-)
[](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280033-why-can-t-i-log-in-to-the-customer-control-panel-)
[](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280033-why-can-t-i-log-in-to-the-customer-control-panel-)

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000268754-what-is-the-customer-control-panel-and-how-do-i-access-it-)

# Are There Any Restrictions on The Content Allowed on My Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000271238-are-there-any-restrictions-on-the-content-allowed-on-my-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Are there any restrictions on the content allowed on my server?**

In general, **Contabo servers are unmanaged root servers.** You have exclusive access to your servers. As long as there are no violations against German law or [Contabo’s Terms of Service (ToS),](https://contabo.com/en/legal/terms-and-conditions/)
 **there are no controls on for what you use your server for.** 

#### **How are abuse complaints handled by Contabo?  
**

Abuse is a serious threat on the Internet and can cause a great deal of damage and must be dealt with strictly and with no tolerance.

If complaints about your server have been sent to Contabo and the complaint includes things that are against the [Contabo ToS](https://contabo.com/en/legal/terms-and-conditions/)
, the customer support team will contact you about the complaint.

You will be asked to take immediate action to stop the ongoing server abuse. It is required that you solve the problem within 24 hours of being notified by the customer support team and that you reply within this period too. Your response must include all information necessary to understand exactly what actions you have taken to stop the abuse and prevent similar incidents in the future.

**If you do not respond to the customer support team confirming that the abuse problem has been resolved within 24 hours, access to your server will be suspended.**

#### **Can I re-activate my server after an abuse complaint?**

**If you do not respond to the customer support abuse notification within 24 hours, it is still possible to reactivate your server.**

**You will need to contact the customer support team to reactivate your server.** You can raise a ticket with the customer support team via the [](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/en/support/home)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
.

**The customer support team will confirm the reactivation price when you contact them for reactivation.**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000271238-are-there-any-restrictions-on-the-content-allowed-on-my-server-)

# What Happens if I Miss a Payment?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000269313-what-happens-if-i-miss-a-payment/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What happens if I miss a payment?**

All services provided by Contabo have a specific renewal date, depending on the contract period and the date you have ordered the service.

It is essential that your payments are received before the renewal date. **Missing payments can lead to a temporary suspension or total cancellation of services.** If you do not contact Contabo regarding a server that has not been paid for, data loss cannot be excluded.

Do not send payments at the last moment, do it a few days before the due date to make sure your payment is processed in time.

It is also recommended to set up recurring payments with credit card or PayPal. This way, you won’t have to worry about paying on time, as the payment will be made automatically and you won’t miss a payment.

#### **Where can I see upcoming payments?  
**

**As an existing customer, you have access to the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
, where you can check upcoming renewal dates at any time.** You will also receive an automated reminder a few days before your renewal date.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000269313-what-happens-if-i-miss-a-payment-)

# Which Tools Are Compatible With Contabo's Object Storage?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000283058-which-tools-are-compatible-with-contabo-s-object-storage/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Which Tools Are Compatible With Contabo’s Object Storage?**

*   [](https://docs.contabo.com/docs/products/Object-Storage/Tools/cntb)
    cntb
*   aws cli
*   rclone
*   Cyberduck
*   goofys
*   s3fs-fuse
*   Nextcloud
*   s3cmd
*   WinSCP
*   Cloudberry Explorer
*   BucketAnywhere
*   Virtualmin
*   Velero
*   Plesk

Contabo’s S3 compatible Object Storage is largely compatible with Amazon’s AWS S3 Object Storage. Not all features of AWS S3 are fully supported. For example, logging is not supported.

Contabo uses [Ceph](https://ceph.com/)
 to provide the S3 functionality. Some tools like [rclone](https://rclone.org/)
 provide direct support for Ceph but some other tools don’t. They usually offer support for other S3 compatible Object Storage providers. 

**For tools not on the list, please see the following information:**

*   For S3 URL and credentials please refer to [S3 Connection Settings](https://docs.contabo.com/docs/products/Object-Storage/s3-connection-settings)
    
*   specify Contabo specific S3 URLs
*   sometimes access\_key is named aws\_access\_key\_id
*   sometimes secret\_key is named aws\_secret\_access\_key
*   you may specify the S3 region default
*   path style buckets are used

#### **Tools known not to work** **with Contabo’s Object Storage:**

*   **FileZillaPro:** FileZillaPro forces users to use a virtual bucket host name, i.e. bucket names in the domain name. As an alternative please use Cyberduck.
*   **cPanel / WHM:** As cPanel / WHM supports specifying a backup destination folder mounting the Object Storage to that backup folder could be a workaround.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000283058-which-tools-are-compatible-with-contabo-s-object-storage-)

# What is the Cyberduck Tool?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305595-what-is-the-cyberduck-tool/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is the Cyberduck tool?**

[Cyberduck](https://cyberduck.io/)
 is a free desktop application for MacOS and Windows which allows users to manage remote files for a wide variety of protocols including S3 compatible Object Storage. 

Please follow the installation instructions described [here](https://cyberduck.io/download/)
. You will need the following information to install Cyberduck:  
  

*   access\_key
*   secret\_key
*   Contabo’s S3 URL

  

#### **How do I configure Cyberduck with Contabo’s Object Storage?**

1.  Create a new bookmark as shown below:
    
    ![New bookmark](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133956874/original/2hQxZCtFi5WwAbpfrLI_DxN2hke_VVi_gw.png?1722323821)
    
2.  Choose Amazon S3 as the type:  
    ![Selecting Amazon S§ as the type from dropdown](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133957269/original/PkCKJyMIgR9K30jfoNUXJiC3aONso6BlTA.png?1722323875)
3.  Enter the following settings:

*   In the field Server enter the Contabo’s S3 URL. Please make sure to enter without https://.
*   In the field Access Key ID enter your access\_key, e.g. 82046e8110804a43bf29c1ae426a724d.
*   In the field Secret Access Key enter your secret\_key, e.g. 82e69bd7a52076c527154297a76c2233.

  

![new bookmark](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103133953426/original/orurBjgB4YET58wOuwSVxChPIUtHJ_MKbg.png?1722323353)

  

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000305595-what-is-the-cyberduck-tool-)

# My Service Was Automatically Renewed, Can I Cancel It?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000327506-my-service-was-automatically-renewed-can-i-cancel-it/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **My service was automatically renewed, can I cancel it?**

If your service is automatically renewed and you missed your chance to cancel your service, a payment will automatically be taken from your account balance.

If you wish to receive a refund for this automatic renewal and cancel your service, **please contact our support team within 72 hours of your service renewal.  
**  
When you contact support, you’ll need to confirm that you accept that any data on your server that you cancel will be lost. You can do this by saying something like “I confirm the complete data loss of my service with the IP address ((add IP here)) and all add-ons”.

  

You can raise a ticket with the customer support team via the [](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/en/support/home)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
.

# How Do I Access Full Monitoring?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000270185-how-do-i-access-full-monitoring/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is the Full Monitoring tool?**

The Full Monitoring tool allows you to monitor your servers and receive notifications if your server suffers an outage.

The Full Monitoring tool is an add-on that can be purchased either with a new server purchase or separately as an existing customer. 

If you are an existing customer and would like to purchase the Full Monitoring add-on, please reach out to the Customer Support team. 

  

You can raise a ticket with the customer support team via the [](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/en/support/home)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
[](https://my.contabo.com/?support=)
.

#### **How do I access the Full Monitoring tool?**

Once you have purchased the Full Monitoring add-on, you will receive a special login for Full Monitoring via the email that you entered during your purchase. 

**Follow the link in the email and you should be able to log in to the Full Monitoring tool.**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270185-how-do-i-access-full-monitoring-)

# Wie installiere ich ein Custom Image (benutzerdefiniertes Bild) auf meinem Server?

[Zum Inhalt springen](https://contabo.com/blog/de/kb/103000274217-wie-installiere-ich-ein-custom-image-benutzerdefiniertes-bild-auf-meinem-server/#content "Zum Inhalt springen")

Hosten Sie Ihren eigenen KI-Agenten mit [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/de/openclaw-hosting/)
 - kostenlose 1-Klick-Installation!

#### **Wie installiere ich ein benutzerdefiniertes Bild auf meinem Server?**

Sobald Sie Ihr benutzerdefiniertes Bild erstellt haben, können Sie es mit den folgenden Schritten über das [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 auf Ihrem Server installieren.

1.  Loggen Sie sich in das [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
     ein.
2.  Klicken Sie auf „Benutzerdefinierte Bilder“ im unteren Abschnitt „Control Panel“ auf der linken Seite.
3.  Klicken Sie auf die Schaltfläche „Benutzerdefiniertes Bild hinzufügen“.
4.  Füllen Sie das Textfeld „Bild-URL“ aus. Dies sollte die URL sein, von der das benutzerdefinierte Bild abgerufen werden soll. Es sollte ein direkter Link zu einer .iso- oder .qcow2image-Datei sein.
5.  Geben Sie im Textfeld „Bildname“ einen Namen für Ihr Bild ein.
6.  Wählen Sie Ihren Betriebssystemtyp (OS) aus dem Dropdown-Menü „OS Type“.
7.  Geben Sie im Textfeld „Version“ die Versionsnummer Ihres benutzerdefinierten Bildes ein.
8.  Optional können Sie im Textfeld „Beschreibung“ eine Beschreibung des benutzerdefinierten Bildes eingeben.
9.  Überprüfen Sie Ihre Einstellungen für das benutzerdefinierte Bild und klicken Sie auf die Schaltfläche „Hochladen“, wenn Sie zufrieden sind. Sie werden benachrichtigt, wenn das benutzerdefinierte Bild hochgeladen wird.

#### **Wie kann ich ein benutzerdefiniertes Bild auf meinem Server verwenden?**

Um Ihr benutzerdefiniertes Bild für einen Cloud-VPS oder VDS zu verwenden, achten Sie bitte darauf, dass es  `Cloud-init-Schalter`in der VPS/VDS-Übersicht grün ist. Klicken Sie dann auf Neuinstallation, dann erscheint ein Bildschirm, in dem Sie das zu verwendende benutzerdefinierte Bild auswählen können.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/de/support/solutions/articles/103000274217-wie-installiere-ich-ein-custom-image-benutzerdefiniertes-bild-auf-meinem-server-)

# How Can I Close My Contabo Account or Cancel a Contabo Service?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000269314-how-can-i-close-my-contabo-account-or-cancel-a-contabo-service/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **How can I cancel a Contabo service?** 

You can cancel all of services in the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 by following these steps:

1.  Open the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click “Your services” in the blue “control panel” section on the left hand side of the page.
3.  Click the manage button next to the service you want to cancel.
4.  Click “Cancel service” in the drop-down menu that opens.

#### **How can I close my Contabo account?**

Each account will be removed after the last product on the account is canceled, **which means you will not be able to log in to the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 if you have no subscriptions left.**

**So if you want to close your Contabo account completely, cancel all your subscriptions and your account will be closed shortly after.**

#### **Can I re-activate my Contabo account after closing it?**

If you wish to purchase Contabo services after cancelling your subscription, **simply order a product as a new customer and a new account will be created for you.**

#### **Can I cancel a newly purchased Contabo product?**

You can request revocation of your Contabo product **within the first 14 days of your Contabo contract.**

There is more information on how to do this [here](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/a/solutions/articles/103000280031?lang=en)
.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000269314-how-can-i-close-my-contabo-account-or-cancel-a-contabo-service-)

# How Does Virtualization Work Exactly? | Contabo Blog

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/how-does-virtualization-work/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![virtualization cover picture](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/Blog-Head_1200x630_Virtualization-1.jpg)

In the [first article](https://contabo.com/blog/how-does-vps-work/)
 of this series, we explained that the existence of virtual instances like VPS is possible thanks to technology called virtualization. But how does virtualization itself work in detail?

In the second part of Cloud Explained Series, we’re going to talk about how a virtual machine is connected to the hardware, what kind of software is necessary to perform virtualization and we’ll mention some of the most popular tools used for virtualization.

What is Virtualization?
-----------------------

To put it simply, virtualization is a process when a physical computer creates another, virtual computer. This virtual computer is what we usually call a [VPS](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
, a virtual machine or cloud instance. It has assigned a certain capacity of the original physical computer. Similar to your normal computer, every instance is equipped with a certain amount of RAM, CPU cores, hard disk, and other resources. Although virtual, the newly created instance can be used to perform the same operations as the original non-virtual computer.

For an easy example from personal computing, look at this screenshot:

![Ubuntu virtualization ORACLE box](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/Ubuntu-virtualization-ORACLE-box.jpg)

As you can see, the desktop clearly belongs to Microsoft Windows. But in the window on the left, the Linux Ubuntu is running. In this case, the host operating system is Microsoft Windows. Then we’ve created a virtual machine equipped with Ubuntu Linux. Ubuntu virtual machine is running on top of Windows. Both virtual and host operating systems are independent of each other and can run simultaneously.

The same principle applies in a commercial setting. The difference is that the host system is running on a physical server somewhere in a data center. You as a customer can order chunks of the physical server capacity in the form of a VPS. How much of this capacity is up to you and your wallet, but modern physical servers are powerful machines, and you most likely use just a fraction of the whole performance. For instance, our biggest VPS XL runs on 10 CPU cores and 60 GB of RAM while the host system has 32 CPU cores and 512 GB of RAM.

Virtual instances are fully functional virtual computers with their own operating system. At Contabo, customers can choose from many operating systems, or even deploy their own custom .iso image:

![Contabo available OS](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/Screenshot-2022-02-01-095535.png)

This is all possible thanks to virtualization.

The idea of splitting one physical server into multiple independent environments goes way back to the sixties and seventies, when engineers from [IBM experimented with so-called time-sharing](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E26996_01/E18549/html/VMUSG1010.html)
. The motivation remains the same – multiple people can use one computer and share its resources.

### Virtual Machines, Virtual Instances and VPS

As confusing as it can be, the IT community uses different terms for labeling virtual computers. Some describe them as virtual machines (VMs), some use the term virtual or cloud instances.

Other terms are used for sales purposes. The term cloud is closely related to virtual machines, describing more of them working together, as we explain later. Individual instances are most often called [VPS](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
. Or the providers use the product name, like Amazon’s EC2 or Digital Ocean’s Droplet.

They all refer to the same concept.

### Virtual Machines and Containers

Both are a hot topic in the IT industry right now. They both use virtualization. But while virtual machines consist of a whole independent virtual operating system, containers are small and portable instances sharing the capacity of a host operating system.

We will talk about containers in the next article of Cloud Explained Series. This article is focused solely on virtual machines.

How Does it work?
-----------------

### Modern-era Virtualization Uses Hypervisors

In the early days of virtualization, a new instance was created using software virtualization on the original operating system. This proved to be less effective and modern virtualization is mostly hardware-based. Hardware resources such as CPUs, RAM and others are allocated dynamically to any number of virtual instances. The software responsible for this distribution is called a hypervisor.

Hypervisor is a software installed directly on the server’s hardware. It is somehow similar to a hardware driver in the OS kernel that can very quickly communicate with the bare metal parts. That makes hypervisor software similar to various drivers that your computer uses to communicate for instance with a printer or with a Wi-Fi card.

There are two kinds of hypervisors – type 1 hypervisor and type 2 hypervisor. The type 1 hypervisor is communicating directly with the hardware while type 2 needs an operating system in-between.

Both hypervisors are still used, but for commercial settings, type 1 is usually deployed because it has a shorter response time. Type 1 hypervisors are also sometimes called “hosted hypervisors”. You may have already heard names like Hyper-V, XEN or Citrix, those are all commercial type 1 hypervisors.

Type 2 hypervisor is suitable for individual users who want to have an extra operating system on top of their default OS. Type 2 is more versatile, making it a perfect tool for software development. Have you noticed the name of the app running our virtual Ubuntu in the first picture? That is Oracle VM VirtualBox, a type 2 hypervisor.

### How Do Hypervisors Manage Hardware Resources?

From customer’s perspective, virtual machines behave in the same manner as if the user is working on a regular non-virtual computer. While other virtual instances are located on the same physical server, their users are not aware of their neighbors.

Hardware capacity for each instance is constantly changing depending on the actual need of the guest OS. Hypervisors will send just enough resources from the hardware to cover the request of a particular virtual machine. Sometimes your virtual machine uses more resources and sometimes your neighbors put a heavy load on the physical server, while you use minimum RAM and CPU capacity. Exactly this principle makes modern virtual instances resource-savvy and hence affordable.

Notable Software for Virtualization
-----------------------------------

In the previous chapter, we’ve explained that you need a special software to create virtual instances, namely a _hypervisor_. Let’s take a quick look at some of the most popular hypervisors today. Because this series is primarily about cloud, we focus on hypervisors you can find in enterprise settings.

All mentioned hypervisors in this chapter are type 1 hypervisors, meaning they are installed directly on the hardware without any operating system in-between.

### Xen (historical)

This pioneer in commercial virtualization was purchased by Citrix in 2007. The Xen Project was developed in late 1990s at Cambridge University and marks one of the milestones of commercial hypervisor deployment, as the [goal](https://www.cl.cam.ac.uk/research/srg/netos/papers/2003-xensosp.pdf)
 of the project was:

"_Our design is targeted at hosting up to 100 virtual machine instances simultaneously on a modern server._"

Computer Laboratory, University of Cambridge

### VMware vSphere

is currently dominating the enterprise sector.

Features:

• Most popular hypervisor on the market  
• Wide array of optimalization for experts  
• Free for testing, costly paid version

### Hyper-V

Microsoft’s own solution focuses on customers that rely entirely on their products without using open-source software.

Features:

• Easy implementation for companies built on Microsoft products  
• Less expensive than vSphere, but also less features

### Citrix

is a low-cost solution for commercial projects. The company is known as an industry leader in the field of 3D graphics virtualization

Features:

• More affordable than Hyper-V and vSphere  
• Perfect for companies using both Linux and Windows  
• Optimized for 3D graphics

### KVM

Is an open source software built directly into Linux operating system, turning the Linux kernel itself into a hypervisor. Although open-source, it’s offered as part of commercial packages, for instance [Red Hat Virtualization Suite](https://www.redhat.com/cms/managed-files/vi-rhv4-feature-guide-inc0421083-201607nb-en.pdf)
.

Features:

• Linux native – easy to integrate and fast for any Linux OS  
• Cost-effective thanks to being open-source

KVM is currently Contabo’s preferred hypervisor, visit chapter 5 for more information

Benefits of Virtualization
--------------------------

### Virtual Machines are Easy to Scale

Because the VPS instance is virtual, it’s very easy to upgrade your hardware capacity, adding more RAM or more CPU cores or more hard disk space. Individual users use just a tiny fraction of the overall provider’s server capacity. Your provider can instantly allocate more capacity from the physical instance.

Contabo, for instance, offers resizing via API and directly in the control panel:

![vps upgrade](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/VPS-upgrade-CCP-2.png)

Scaling up your VPS instance does not interrupt your data in any way. The upgrade is seamless and immediate, the only thing that changes is the new specifications.

Note that we are not only talking about changing parameters coming from the same physical server, but also about the ability to move instances to a different physical server. A provider can move any instance to another piece of hardware without stopping any workload.

### Virtual Machines are Affordable

The price tag for VPS is many times lower than for a dedicated server. While you can purchase a VPS for €3.99 at Contabo, a bare metal server from our standard line will cost you at least €139.

With the rise of VPS, even small businesses and individuals can have the independence that comes with a private virtual server, their own IP address and their own operating system.

That being said, it would be wrong to understand VPS only as a low-end solution. Large corporations sometimes buy VPS in bulk to build very complex cloud setups. Each server typically has just one specific type of task to execute, and the result is passed on via API to the next instance which performs another task. This way it’s easy to scale tasks that are becoming bottlenecks. It’s also a better setup from a security and debugging perspective. Sometimes these clusters can get very big. Our biggest customers use thousands of VPS simultaneously.

### Virtual Machines are Safe

Each of the virtual machines runs in a separate environment independent of other instances. For that very reason, developers use virtual machines as safe sandboxes when testing new things or interacting with potentially malicious content. When something goes wrong, other instances are intact.

But the independence of each VPS is also important not only for developers. In case one instance fails or is attacked by a hacker, the rest of the network will remain unaffected and the business will still run as usual.

Virtualization at Contabo
-------------------------

We use hypervisor software KVM – Kernel-based Virtual Machine as a standard hypervisor. KVM allows near bear-metal performance and support of so-called para-virtualization drivers. These drivers are designated to enhance performance of guests who run heavy tasks and applications.

![Tino profile picture](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/MicrosoftTeams-image-17-edited-1.jpg)

**In-house expert Tino Lehnig, Cloud Architect**

_“We hold KVM in high regard not only for its performance, but also for the flexibility. Our Linux-based KVM host system allows us to build our own bespoke orchestration tools to facilitate software deployments, performance monitoring and automated load balancing.”_ – says Tino Lehnig. “_Over the years, we have built several custom tools to make Contabo customers’ experience smooth as possible._”

Contabo has implemented measures to balance the load on physical servers to provide customers stable performance. We measure performance of our host servers in real time to identify servers which are near limit of the server’s capacity. If such a situation occurs, we migrate some [virtual machines](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
 from one server a to another that has more resources available at the moment– without that particular user even noticing.

# Contabo API for Easy Hosting Management

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/contabo-api-server-management/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![Watch how to use the Contabo API for easy hosting management](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/How-to-Access-Contabo-API.png "How to Access Contabo API")

Have you ever found yourself repeatedly logging into the Customer Control Panel for routine server management tasks? Or perhaps you’re looking to automate your hosting infrastructure as your operations grow? Many server administrators find themselves in this position as they scale their online presence. 

The Contabo API solves these common challenges by allowing you to manage your hosting resources programmatically. Instead of manual interventions for each change, the API lets you streamline workflows while maintaining complete control over your infrastructure. 

Watch Our Complete API Tutorial 
--------------------------------

Why the Contabo API Matters for Your Business 
----------------------------------------------

When managing your hosting infrastructure, our API offers significant benefits: 

*   **Efficient Resource Management**   
    Control all aspects of your hosting resources using simple HTTP requests, no need to log in to the Customer Control Panel each time. 

*   **Zero Downtime Integration**   
    Activate and use the API without any service interruptions to your existing infrastructure. 

*   **Global Accessibility**   
    Access and manage your resources from anywhere in the world with consistent performance. 

*   **Automation Capabilities**   
    Deploy new servers, manage storage, or monitor performance through programmatic workflows. 

Setting Up API Access in the Contabo Control Panel 
---------------------------------------------------

Getting started with our API is straightforward: 

1.  **Log in** to the Contabo Customer Control Panel. 

2.  Navigate to the **API section** on the left-hand side under the blue Control Panel menu. 

3.  Locate your credentials:  your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** will be displayed here. 

4.  Set up an API password: 

*   Click **‘Send Link’** to receive an email with setup instructions. 

*   Follow the link in the email to create your API password. 

Practical Applications for the Contabo API 
-------------------------------------------

Here are real-world scenarios where our API solves common challenges: 

*   **Backend for PaaS or SaaS**   
    Easily run your cloud infrastructure and benefit from our global availability and competitive pricing. 

*   **Reseller Operations**   
    Automate the entire order and provisioning process to get resources to your customers faster. 

*   **DevOps Integration**   
    Automate core parts of your DevOps pipeline to build applications from code whenever needed. 

*   **Multi-User Management**   
    Allow multiple team members or automated systems to interact with the API simultaneously making resource management more flexible and scalable.

Understanding API Capabilities 
-------------------------------

Our comprehensive API documentation provides detailed information about: 

*   **Instance Management**   
    Deploy and control all aspects of Cloud VPS & VDS via RESTful HTTP requests. 

*   **Secrets Management**   
    Securely store and use passwords and public SSH keys with your cloud resources. 

*   **User Management**   
    Add and manage users with specific access rights and permissions. 

*   **Tag Management**   
    Organize your cloud resources efficiently with customizable tags. 

Ready to Streamline Your Hosting Management? 
---------------------------------------------

The Contabo API gives you the **flexibility to grow and adapt** your online services as your needs evolve. The setup process is straightforward, and the benefits to **efficiency, automation, and management** are immediate. 

**Start managing your hosting programmatically today** and focus on what matters most: building and growing your projects.

# How Can I Change My Personal Details?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000273224-how-can-i-change-my-personal-details/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Can I change my personal details?**

**It is possible to change some personal details through the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
.** See below for a quick guide on how to do this below.

#### **How can I change my personal details?**

1.  Log in to the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click “Customer details” in the blue “control panel” box on the left hand side of the page.
3.  You should be taken to the customer details page. You can then make any changes to your personal details in the available text boxes. **Please note that you cannot make changes to your name, your companies name or your country, there is more information on how to make these types of changes in the section below.**
4.  Once you’ve made your changes, click the “Save” button and you should see your changes immediately. 

#### **How can I change my name, company name or country?**

**The only details that you cannot change yourself with the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 are your name, your companies name and your country. To make these changes to your personal details you will need to reach out to the customer support team.**

# How Do I Boot a Rescue System For My Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000295053-how-do-i-boot-a-rescue-system-for-my-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Whats is a rescue system?**

A rescue system allows you to partially access your server and fix any problems that may be preventing you from accessing it normally.

#### **How do I boot a rescue system for my server?**

You can boot a rescue system (like a live CD) into the RAM of your server if you need access and are unable to do so through normal methods. This option is available for Virtual Private Servers (VPS), Virtual Dedicated Servers (VDS) and Dedicated Servers.

It allows you to access the system, make changes, fix errors, and reboot the system normally. This option is free of charge and you can do it yourself right now using the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
. Please follow the steps below to try using a rescue system:

1.  Log in to the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click “Your services” from the control panel on the left hand side of the page.
3.  Click the “Manage” button next to the affected server.
4.  Click the “Rescue System” option.
5.  Select the type of rescue system you want to run from the drop-down list. Details of each recovery system can be seen at the top of the page.
6.  Type the server password in the “Password” text box.
7.  Re-enter the server password in the “Password (confirm)” text box.
8.  Click “Start rescue system”

After a short wait, you should be able to access your server via SSH using the server IP and default port 22. The root password is the one you just entered.

This option is best used if you know the cause of your server’s problem and need to regain access to fix it.

We recommend PuTTy (command line) and WinSCP (file transfer) for Windows clients.

#### **How do I access data and files after booting a rescue system on my server?** 

If you want to access data and files after booting a rescue system on your server, you need to follow the steps below, which include running some Linux commands:

1.  Enter the below command to make sure you have admin rights:
    
    sudo -i
    
2.  Next run the below command to check which partitions are available:
    
        fdisk -l
        
    
3.  The root file system is usually located on /dev/sda2 or /dev/vda2. Use the following command to mount the partition:
    
     mount /dev/sda2 /mnt/ 
    
4.  Your server files are now available in /mnt/.
5.  If you use a Windows as operating system on your server, our rescue system can be helpful despite the different architecture. The C: drive is usually located on /dev/sda2 or /dev/vda2. The command to mount it with write access is a bit different:
    
    ntfs-3g /dev/sda2 /mnt/
    
6.  You are now set to modify configuration files or to retrieve personal data. WinSCP is perfect for both of those purposes.
7.  When you have finished your work and want to boot your server normally again, enter the following command:
    
    reboot
    

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000295053-how-do-i-boot-a-rescue-system-for-my-server-)
