# How to Set Up Cloudflare for Your Website: A Step-by-Step Guide | Contabo Blog

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/blog-head_how2-enable-cloudflare.jpg)

Have you ever visited a website that loads in the blink of an eye, no matter where you are in the world? Chances are, it’s using Cloudflare. This powerful tool can make your website faster, more secure, and more reliable. But if you’re new to the world of Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) and website security, Cloudflare might seem a bit difficult to understand.

Whether you’re running a small blog, an e-commerce store, or a large corporate website, this guide will walk you through enabling Cloudflare for your site. We’ll cover everything from the basics of what Cloudflare is, to the ins and outs of setting it up and optimizing your settings.

Ready to give your website the Cloudflare advantage? Let’s find out how this game-changing technology can take your site to the next level.

Article Topics
--------------

*   [What is Cloudflare and Why Use It?](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-what-is-cloudflare-and-why-use-it)
    
*   [Benefits of Using Cloudflare for Your Website](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-benefits-of-using-cloudflare-for-your-website)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Cloudflare](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-step-by-step-guide-to-setting-up-cloudflare)
    
*   [How to Configure DNS Settings in Cloudflare](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-how-to-configure-dns-settings-in-cloudflare)
    
*   [Enhancing Security with SSL/TLS Settings](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-enhancing-security-with-ssl-tls-settings)
    
*   [Optimizing Website Speed with Cloudflare’s CDN](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-optimizing-website-speed-with-cloudflare-s-cdn)
    
*   [Monitoring and Analyzing Website Traffic](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-monitoring-and-analyzing-website-traffic)
    
*   [Common Setup Issues and How to Fix Them](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-common-setup-issues-and-how-to-fix-them)
    
*   [Final Thoughts on Using Cloudflare for Your Website](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-final-thoughts-on-using-cloudflare-for-your-website)
    
*   [Related Articles](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-set-up-cloudflare-for-your-website-a-step-by-step-guide/#h-related-articles)
    

What is Cloudflare and Why Use It?
----------------------------------

Cloudflare is a service that acts as a protective layer between your website and the internet. It’s like having a smart traffic controller for your site, optimizing how data travels between your server and your visitors.

Here’s what it does:

1.  **Website Speed Optimization:** It caches your website content across its global network, helping your site load faster for visitors worldwide.
2.  **Security Enhancement:** Cloudflare filters out malicious traffic before it reaches your server, protecting against various online threats.
3.  **SSL/TLS Encryption:** It offers free SSL certificates, ensuring secure connections for your visitors.
4.  **Load Balancing:** During traffic spikes, Cloudflare distributes the load across multiple servers, improving performance.
5.  **Analytics:** It provides insights into your website traffic, helping you understand and optimize your online presence.

By using this service, you’re not just improving your website’s performance; you’re also enhancing its security and reliability. This can lead to better user experience, improved search engine rankings, and potentially increased conversions for your business.

![](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/what-is-cloudflare@2x.png)

Cloudflare can be a valuable addition to web hosting setups of all shapes and sizes. It works seamlessly with various hosting solutions to provide an extra layer of protection and performance for your website.

In the following sections, we’ll explore how to set up this service for your website and make the most of its features.

Benefits of Using Cloudflare for Your Website
---------------------------------------------

Implementing Cloudflare can significantly enhance your website’s performance and security. Let’s explore the key advantages:

1.  **Analytics and Insights:**  
    Cloudflare provides detailed web traffic analytics, helping you understand visitor behavior.
2.  **Improved Website Speed:**  
    A global network of data centers caches your content, delivering it to visitors from the nearest location. This reduces latency and improves load times, creating a better user experience.
3.  **Enhanced Cybersecurity:**  
    The service acts as a shield against various online threats, offering domain protection against DDoS attacks and malicious bots. It filters out potentially harmful traffic before it reaches your server, adding an extra layer of security.
4.  **SSL/TLS Encryption:**  
    Cloudflare provides free SSL certificates, ensuring secure connections between your visitors and your website. This not only protects user data but can also improve your search engine rankings.
5.  **Load Balancing:**  
    During traffic spikes, Cloudflare distributes the load across multiple servers, helping maintain your site’s performance even under heavy traffic conditions.
6.  **Cost-Effectiveness:**  
    By reducing the load on your origin server, Cloudflare can help lower your hosting costs. It’s particularly beneficial for websites hosted on plans with limited bandwidth.

These benefits make Cloudflare a valuable addition to your web hosting setup, regardless of your site’s size or purpose. Whether you’re running a small blog on a Contabo [VPS](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
, powering a popular app on a [VDS](https://contabo.com/en/vds/)
, or managing a large e-commerce platform on a [Dedicated Server](https://contabo.com/en/dedicated-servers/)
, Cloudflare can help optimize your site’s performance and security.

Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Cloudflare
-------------------------------------------

Setting up Cloudflare for your website is a straightforward process. Follow these steps to get started:

1.  **Create a Cloudflare account:**  
    Visit the [Cloudflare website](https://www.cloudflare.com/)
     and sign up for a free account.
2.  **Add your website:**  
    Once logged in, click “Add a Site” and enter your domain name.
3.  **Select a plan:**  
    Choose the plan that best fits your needs. The free plan offers basic features suitable for many websites.
4.  **Review DNS records:**  
    Cloudflare will scan and import your existing DNS records. Review them to ensure everything is correct.
5.  **Update your nameservers:**  
    Cloudflare will provide you with new nameservers. Log into your domain registrar and replace your current nameservers with the ones provided.
6.  **Wait for propagation:**  
    DNS changes can take up to 24 hours to propagate globally, though it’s often much quicker.
7.  **Verify and optimize:**  
    Once the nameservers are updated, Cloudflare will send you an email. Log back into your dashboard to verify everything is working and start optimizing your Cloudflare settings.

![](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/setting-up-cloudflare-steps@2x-2400x361.png)

Remember, while Cloudflare works with various hosting solutions, using a reliable hosting provider is essential for optimal performance. If you’re looking for a powerful hosting platform to support your setup, a [VPS](https://contabo.com/en/vps/)
 from Contabo is a great place to start.

In the next section, we’ll learn how to configure your DNS settings for optimal performance and security.

How to Configure DNS Settings in Cloudflare
-------------------------------------------

Once you’ve set up Cloudflare for your website, configuring your DNS settings is the next step. Proper DNS management ensures your site is accessible and secure.

Here’s how to do it:

1.  **Access DNS settings:**  
    Log into your Cloudflare dashboard and select your domain. Click on the “DNS” tab.
2.  **Review existing records:**  
    Cloudflare should have imported your existing DNS records. Double-check that all records are present and correct.
3.  **Add new records:**  
    To add a new record, click “Add record”. You can create various types of DNS records:
    *   A Record: Maps a domain to an IPv4 address
    *   AAAA Record: Maps a domain to an IPv6 address
    *   CNAME Record: Creates an alias for another domain name
    *   MX Record: Specifies mail servers for your domain
4.  **Enable Cloudflare proxy:**  
    For each record, you’ll see an orange cloud icon. When it’s orange, it means Cloudflare is proxying that record, providing security and performance benefits.
5.  **Configure SSL/TLS:**  
    In the SSL/TLS section, choose your encryption mode. “Flexible” is a good starting point, but “Full” or “Full (strict)” offer better security.
6.  **Set up Page Rules:**  
    Use Page Rules to customize Cloudflare’s behavior for specific URLs. This is useful for things like forcing HTTPS or setting cache behaviors.

DNS changes can take some time to propagate globally. Be patient if you don’t see immediate results. Remember, by properly configuring your DNS settings, you’re laying the groundwork for a faster, more secure website. Let’s find out more about how to enhance security in the next section.

Enhancing Security with SSL/TLS Settings
----------------------------------------

One of the key benefits of using Cloudflare is the ability to enhance your website’s security through SSL/TLS encryption. Let’s learn how to configure these settings for optimal protection:

1.  **Access SSL/TLS settings:**  
    Log into your Cloudflare dashboard, select your domain, and click on the “SSL/TLS” tab.
2.  **Choose your SSL/TLS encryption mode:**
    *   Flexible: Encrypts traffic between visitors and Cloudflare, but not between Cloudflare and your server.
    *   Full: Encrypts both connections, but doesn’t verify your server’s SSL certificate.
    *   Full (strict): The most secure option, encrypting all traffic and verifying your server’s SSL certificate.
3.  **Enable Automatic HTTPS Rewrites:**  
    This feature automatically changes “http” to “https” for all resources or links on your site that can be served with HTTPS.
4.  **Configure TLS version:**  
    Choose the minimum TLS version you want to support. TLS 1.2 is recommended for most websites.
5.  **Enable HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)**:  
    This tells browsers to always use HTTPS for your domain, providing an extra layer of security.
6.  **Consider enabling authenticated origin pulls:**  
    This ensures requests to your origin server come only from Cloudflare, adding another security layer.

By properly configuring these settings, you’re significantly enhancing your website’s security posture. Read on to find out how to optimize your site’s speed.

Optimizing Website Speed with Cloudflare’s CDN
----------------------------------------------

One of the key benefits of using Cloudflare is its Content Delivery Network (CDN), which can significantly improve your website’s loading speed. Here’s how to make the most of this feature:

1.  **Enable Cloudflare’s CDN:**  
    This is typically on by default, but you can verify in your Cloudflare dashboard under the “Speed” tab.
2.  **Configure caching:**  
    Set up caching rules to determine how long Cloudflare should store your content. For static content that doesn’t change often, you can set longer cache times.
3.  **Enable Auto Minify:**  
    This feature automatically reduces the file size of your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, speeding up load times.
4.  **Activate Brotli compression:**  
    This advanced compression algorithm can make your files even smaller than traditional gzip compression.
5.  **Use Cloudflare’s Argo Smart Routing:**  
    This paid feature optimizes the path data takes through the internet, often resulting in faster load times.
6.  **Implement Railgun:**  
    Another paid feature, Railgun, can dramatically speed up dynamic content delivery.

Remember, while these optimizations can significantly improve your site’s performance, the foundation of a fast website is still good hosting.

Monitoring and Analyzing Website Traffic
----------------------------------------

One of the lesser-known but very valuable features of Cloudflare is its analytics capabilities. These tools can provide deep insights into your website’s performance and visitor behavior.

Here’s how to make the most of them:

1.  **Access Analytics:**  
    Log into your Cloudflare dashboard and click on the “Analytics” tab.
2.  **Traffic Overview:**  
    Here you’ll find a summary of your website’s traffic, including total requests, data transferred, and unique visitors.
3.  **Security Insights:**  
    Cloudflare provides data on threats it has mitigated, including bot attacks and potential DDoS attempts.
4.  **Performance Metrics:**  
    You can view your site’s cache hit ratio, which indicates how effectively Cloudflare is caching your content.
5.  **Content Analysis:**  
    See which of your pages are most popular and which file types are requested most frequently.
6.  **Geographic Data:**  
    Understand where your visitors are coming from, which can help in content localization efforts.
7.  **DNS Queries:**  
    If you’re using Cloudflare’s DNS, you can monitor query patterns and potential issues.

While Cloudflare’s analytics are powerful, they shouldn’t replace your primary web analytics tool (like Google Analytics). Instead, use them in conjunction for a more complete picture of your site’s performance.

| Metric | What It Tells You | Why It’s Important |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Total Requests | Overall traffic volume | Understand your site’s popularity |
| Cache Hit Ratio | Effectiveness of content caching | Indicates potential for speed improvements |
| Threat Mitigations | Security incidents blocked | Assess the level of protection Cloudflare provides |
| Bandwidth Usage | Amount of data transferred | Monitor resource consumption and costs |
| Status Codes | Distribution of HTTP response codes | Identify potential errors or issues |
| Top Countries | Geographic distribution of traffic | Understand your global audience |
| SSL/TLS Encryption Type | Types of SSL/TLS connections used | Ensure secure connections for visitors |
| Bot Traffic | Percentage of traffic from bots | Differentiate between human and automated traffic |

By gaining key insights from these analytics, you can make data-driven decisions to improve your website’s performance and security.

Common Setup Issues and How to Fix Them
---------------------------------------

Even with careful implementation, you might encounter some hiccups when setting up Cloudflare. Here are some common issues and their solutions:

1.  **DNS Not Propagating:**
    *   Issue: Your site isn’t accessible or is showing old content after switching to Cloudflare.
    *   Fix: Wait for DNS propagation (can take up to 24 hours). If issues persist, double-check your DNS records in Cloudflare.
2.  **SSL/HTTPS Not Working:**
    *   Issue: Your site shows as insecure or SSL errors appear.
    *   Fix: Ensure SSL/TLS encryption is properly set up in Cloudflare. Try changing the SSL/TLS encryption mode to Flexible, then gradually move to Full or Full (Strict).
3.  **Site Loading Slowly:**
    *   Issue: Your website performance hasn’t improved or has worsened.
    *   Fix: Check your caching settings and enable Auto Minify for HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Also, verify that you’re not bypassing the cache for dynamic content unnecessarily.
4.  **Email Delivery Problems:**
    *   Issue: Emails from your domain aren’t being delivered.
    *   Fix: Ensure your MX records are correctly set up in Cloudflare’s DNS settings. You might need to temporarily disable Cloudflare for your mail subdomain.
5.  **Origin Server Not Responding:**
    *   Issue: Cloudflare reports that your origin server is down.
    *   Fix: Check your server’s status and ensure it’s accessible. Check your firewall rules to verify that Cloudflare’s IP ranges are allowlisted.

Remember, many issues can be resolved by carefully reviewing your Cloudflare settings and ensuring they align with your server configuration.

Final Thoughts on Using Cloudflare for Your Website
---------------------------------------------------

As we’ve explored throughout this guide, Cloudflare offers a powerful set of tools to enhance your website’s performance, security, and reliability. Whether you’re running a small blog or managing a large e-commerce platform, integrating Cloudflare can significantly improve your online presence.

Key takeaways:

*   Cloudflare’s CDN can dramatically improve your site’s loading speed.
*   The security features protect your site from various online threats.
*   Analytics tools provide valuable insights into your traffic and performance.
*   While powerful, Cloudflare requires careful configuration to maximize its benefits.

By making the most of Cloudflare’s capabilities alongside a solid hosting infrastructure, you’re well-equipped to deliver a fast, secure, and reliable experience to your website visitors.

# How Do I Enable/Disable VNC for My Server?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000337806-how-do-i-enable-disable-vnc-for-my-server/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Overview:**

You can quickly enable or disable VNC access to your server with the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
. This guide will walk you through the steps to enable or disable VNC as needed.

#### **Table of Contents:**

*   [How do I enable VNC on my server?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000337806-how-do-i-enable-disable-vnc-for-my-server/#How-do-I-enable-VNC-on-my-server)
    
*   [How do I disable VNC on my server?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000337806-how-do-i-enable-disable-vnc-for-my-server/#How-do-I-disable-VNC-on-my-server)
    

#### **How do I enable VNC on my server?**

1.  Login to the [Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click on “Your services” from the blue “control panel” table on the left hand side of the page:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194933582/original/tpbB5fHwFp7hax51KkjH7gxhN1IbKYYslg.png?1741084324)
    
3.  Click the “Manage” button next to the server you want to enable VNC for:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194951300/original/V7QTgY2NI1hN_ZjfdKo0yxAufVdjF5iahQ.png?1741085816)
    
4.  From the drop-down that opens, click “Control”:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194935128/original/mymQ3k7w5kz-OTrBy77her4ovJdLUOTzmA.png?1741084449)
    
5.  You should be taken to the VPS/VDS control page. Click the blue “Manage” button next to the server you want to enable VNC for:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194948463/original/Dmsu3gwTTQbew_TxtXs_1oh_hyNKWoCBSg.png?1741085554)
    
6.  Click “Enable VNC” from the dropdown-menu:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194945450/original/qOrdYGZD0ykukzENY1DC9I7EdQlAWey3-A.png?1741085303)
    
7.  A new window will open asking you for confirmation to enable the VNC. If you’re happy to continue, click the “Enable” button:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194949830/original/vS4d-S0GZz-zUwXIjCdse2OFyXTg4_8AyA.png?1741085668)
    
8.  Another window will open telling you that the VNC access has been enabled but you will need to reboot the server for this change to be applied:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194954921/original/MAIqBLKvb7xgrRbGBbpEyb-ojhZCazxSKA.png?1741086142)
    
9.  Finally, reboot your server. If you’re not sure how to do that you can follow our [guide here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270047-how-can-i-reboot-my-server-)
    .

#### **How do I disable VNC on my server?**

1.  Login to the [Customer Control Panel here](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Click on “Your services” from the blue “control panel” table on the left hand side of the page:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194933582/original/tpbB5fHwFp7hax51KkjH7gxhN1IbKYYslg.png?1741084324)
    
3.  Click the “Manage” button next to the server you want to disable VNC for:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194951628/original/bvkSb7DgCXBLpnWBMLpTIbhCZDdoAOW0hg.png?1741085840)
    
4.  From the drop-down that opens, click “Control”:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194935128/original/mymQ3k7w5kz-OTrBy77her4ovJdLUOTzmA.png?1741084449)
    
5.  You should be taken to the VPS/VDS control page. Click the blue “Manage” button next to the server you want to disable VNC for:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194950135/original/PUXbTuaVU9bGSgtMksYPBL8IOuchdX4gRA.png?1741085702)
    
6.  Click “Disable VNC” from the dropdown-menu:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194938363/original/CMetHnK8L9kaT4dmkNtX8lJKjTQYpHKdGQ.png?1741084737)
    
7.  A new window will open asking you for confirmation to disable the VNC. If you’re happy to continue, click the “Disable” button:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194951946/original/eu76aNZqLJGSHn4vrRrWbcBAqjz6lshQvA.png?1741085870)
    
8.  Another window will open telling you that the VNC access has been disabled but you will need to reboot the server for this change to be applied:  
    ![](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103194941461/original/dYkoFr_9RTEB3j8LOYQcshjKPFvT5K_xag.png?1741084999)
    
9.  Finally, reboot your server. If you’re not sure how to do that you can follow our [guide here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270047-how-can-i-reboot-my-server-)
    .

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000337806-how-do-i-enable-disable-vnc-for-my-server-)

# Debian 10 Is Reaching End Of Life, What Do I Do?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000296348-debian-10-is-reaching-end-of-life-what-do-i-do/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Debian 10 is reaching end of life, what do I do?**

Debian 10 ´reached its EOL on June 2024 and we strongly recommend you to upgrade instances with this version to a newer operating system.

Not upgrading your system will mean your server will be vulnerable as Debian 10 will not longer be supported or receive security updates. 

Depending on whether you use Debian 10 with or without Plesk our recommendations for upgrading from Debian 10 are slightly different. Below you can find how we recommend you upgrade your server based on these options.

#### **I’m using Debian 10 with Plesk, what do I do?**

If you’re using Debian 10 with Plesk it is recommend to perform a dist upgrade, you can find a [guide for this here](https://support.plesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/12377714344983-How-to-perform-dist-upgrade-procedure-on-Linux-server-with-Plesk)
.

Before making the upgrade we recommend making a backup or snapshot of your server to make sure no data is lost. You can find a guide on [how to create a snapshot here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270385-how-do-i-create-a-snapshot-of-my-server-)
 and [how to create a backup here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000281488-how-do-i-create-a-backup-using-the-contabo-backup-space-)
.

[](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270385-how-do-i-create-a-snapshot-of-my-server-)
[](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000281488-how-do-i-create-a-backup-using-the-contabo-backup-space-)

#### **I’m using Debian 10 without Plesk, what do I do?**

If you’re using Debain 10 without Plesk we recommend upgrading to Debian 11 with the below steps.

Also to note Debian 11 will only receive security updates from late August 2024 so we also recommend upgrading to Debian 12. To do this you first must upgrade to Debian 11 with the steps below. You can then follow our [guide here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000296654--draft-do-not-use-how-do-i-upgrade-my-server-from-debian-11-to-debian-12)
 where you can upgrade from Debian 11 to Debian 12.

1.  Make a snapshot or backup of your server to make sure no data is lost. You can find a guide on [how to create a snapshot here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000270385-how-do-i-create-a-snapshot-of-my-server-)
     and a guide on [how to create a backup here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000281488-how-do-i-create-a-backup-using-the-contabo-backup-space-)
    .
2.  Check that there are no packages in hold with the following command:
    
        sudo apt-mark showhold | more
    
    If no packages are returned you can move on to the next step. If the command does return some packages in hold you will need to remove the hold status from them with the following command:
    
        sudo apt-mark unhold [package_name]
    
3.  Next you need to make sure all packages and repositories are up to date by running the following command:
    
    sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
    
4.  Make sure the gcc-8-base package is installed with the following command:
    
        sudo apt install gcc-8-base
    
    If the package isn’t installed the installation should begin. If the package is already installed nothing should happen and you can move on to the next step.
    
5.  Next you will need to update the APT’s source list files. First change the directory to etc/apt with the following command:
    
        cd /etc/apt
    
6.  Open the _sources.list_ file using your preferred text editor. In the example we will use nano: 
    
    sudo nano sources.list
    
7.  Using the text editor remove any sources that contain the word “buster”. 
8.  Add the following new sources with the text editor:_deb http://deb.debian.org/debian bullseye main contrib non-free  
    deb http://deb.debian.org/debian bullseye-updates main contrib non-free  
    deb http://security.debian.org/debian-security bullseye-security main  
    deb http://ftp.debian.org/debian bullseye-backports main contrib non-free_
9.  Run the following command to update the system repositories:
    
        sudo apt update
    
10.  Run the following command to update to Debian 11:
    
    sudo apt full-upgrade -y
    
11.  When prompted to restart service packages, select Yes using the arrow keys and press Enter.
12.  Run the following command to restart the system to complete the upgrade process:  
    
    systemctl reboot
    
13.  After the reboot you can check the version by running this command:
    
        cat /etc/debian_version
    

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000296348-debian-10-is-reaching-end-of-life-what-do-i-do-)

# How Do I Access the Private Networking Feature?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000274383-how-do-i-access-the-private-networking-feature/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **What is Private Networking?**

Private Networking is an optional extension to the functionality of Contabo’s Virtual Private Servers (VPS) and Virtual Dedicated Servers (VDS) that allows the activated instances in the same Contabo location to communicate within a private network. **The maximum network size is /22, which provides a maximum of 1024 addresses,** and a server can be added to several private networks at the same time. 

**The Private Networking feature is available to servers in all regions,** but private networks can only be created in one region at a time. **Only instances in the same data center can be added to a private network.**

Instances within the same private network are assigned private IP addresses and communication between them is restricted to that private network.

#### **How can I buy the Private Networking feature for a new Contabo server?**

1.  Go to the [Contabo website](https://contabo.com/)
    .
2.  Select the VPS or VDS you would like to buy.
3.  You should be taken to the “Configure your VPS/VDS” page. Configure your VPS/VDS as you would like.
4.  You should see a section called “Networking”. Set the “Private Networking” dropdown to “Private Networking Enabled”.
5.  Complete the purchase of your new Contabo server. 

#### **How Can I buy the Private Networking feature for an existing VPS or VDS?**

1.  Log in to the [](https://my.contabo.com/)
    [](https://my.contabo.com/)
    [Contabo Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    .  
    
2.  Click on “Private Networking” from the blue “control panel” section on the left hand side of the page.
3.  Click on the “Create new Private Network” button.
4.  Select the region from the “Region” dropdown list.
5.  Select the server you would like to purchase the Private Networking feature for.
6.  Click the “Continue” button.
7.  You will be shown how much you will have to pay monthly for the Private Networking feature. Once you’re satisfied click the “Order and Pay to continue”.

#### **How do I get access to the Private Networking features?**

After purchasing the Private Networking add-on for a Contabo instance, the features are directly available in the Contabo Control Panel. Just click on “Private Networking” from the blue “control panel” section on the left hand side of the page and you should be able to set up your Private Network.

#### **What are the benefits I get from an instance with Private Networking enabled?**

*   Secure communication between Contabo instances within the same location – connect multiple Cloud VPS / Cloud VDS through a private network to enable secure data transfer between them.
*   Auto-assigned private IP addresses for effortless configuration – just choose the relevant Cloud VPS / Cloud VDS and private network then we’ll do the rest, no manual selection of network sizes / masks or manual private IP configuration.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000274383-how-do-i-access-the-private-networking-feature-)

# Wie kann ich meine persönlichen Daten ändern?

[Zum Inhalt springen](https://contabo.com/blog/de/kb/103000273224-wie-kann-ich-meine-persoenlichen-daten-aendern/#content "Zum Inhalt springen")

Hosten Sie Ihren eigenen KI-Agenten mit [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/de/openclaw-hosting/)
 - kostenlose 1-Klick-Installation!

#### **Kann ich meine persönlichen Daten ändern?**

Es ist möglich, einige persönliche Daten über das [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 zu ändern. Eine kurze Anleitung dazu finden Sie unten.

#### **Wie kann ich meine persönlichen Daten ändern?**

1.  Melden Sie sich beim [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
     an.
2.  Klicken Sie auf „Kundendaten“ in dem blauen Feld „Bedienfeld“ auf der linken Seite.
3.  Sie werden dann auf die Seite mit den Kundendaten weitergeleitet. Dort können Sie in den verfügbaren Textfeldern Änderungen an Ihren persönlichen Daten vornehmen. Bitte beachten Sie, dass Sie keine Änderungen an Ihrem Namen, Ihrem Firmennamen oder Ihrem Land vornehmen können. Weitere Informationen dazu, wie Sie diese Änderungen vornehmen, finden Sie im folgenden Abschnitt.
4.  Sobald Ihre Änderungen abgeschlossen sind, klicken Sie auf die Schaltfläche „Speichern“ und Ihre Änderungen sollten sofort sichtbar sein.

#### **Wie kann ich meinen Namen, meinen Firmennamen oder mein Land ändern?**

Die einzigen Angaben, die Sie nicht selbst über das [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
 ändern können, sind Ihr Name, der Name Ihres Unternehmens und Ihr Land. Um diese Änderungen an Ihren persönlichen Daten vorzunehmen, müssen Sie sich an das Kundensupport-Team wenden.

Sie können das Kunden-Support-Team über den Bereich „Nehmen Sie Kontakt mit uns auf“ auf der Support-Homepage [hier kontaktieren](https://help.contabo.com/de/support/home)
 .

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/de/support/solutions/articles/103000273224-wie-kann-ich-meine-pers%C3%B6nlichen-daten-%C3%A4ndern-)

# Which Currencies Are Accepted by Contabo?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000269311-which-currencies-are-accepted-by-contabo/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Overview of currencies at Contabo:**

There are a number of currencies and payment methods accepted by Contabo that do not depend on your location or the location of your server. When you purchase a product, you choose the currency in which you wish to pay for Contabo’s services.

It is also possible to change your payment currency after purchasing a Contabo product, you can find a guide on how to do that [here](https://contabo.freshdesk.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000226598--draft-do-not-use-how-to-change-my-billing-currency)
.

#### **List of available currencies and payment methods:**

Here is a list of all the available currencies you can pay with:

**EUR (€)**

Credit Card  
PayPal  
Skrill  
Bank Transfer  
Direct Debit (for qualifying customers)

**USD ($)**  
Credit Card  
PayPal

**GBP (£)**  
Credit Card  
PayPal

**Payments in cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin are not accepted.**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000269311-which-currencies-are-accepted-by-contabo-)

# What is the Rclone Tool?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Table of Contents  
**

*   [What is the Rclone tool?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool/#What-is-the-Rclone-tool)
    
*   [How do I install rclone?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool/#How-do-I-install-rclone)
    
*   [How do I configure rclone with Contabo’s Object Storage?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool/#How-do-I-configure-rclone-with-Contabo)
    
*   [How do I mount using rclone?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool/#How-do-I-mount-using-rclone)
    

#### **What is the Rclone tool?**

[Rclone](https://rclone.org/)
 is a free tool for managing files and folders on cloud storage on Windows, macOS, Linux and FreeBSD. 

#### **How do I install rclone?**

Please follow the installation instructions described [here](https://rclone.org/downloads/)
. We recommend using the latest stable release from there. 

Before installing rclone you will need the following information:

*   access\_key
*   secret\_key
*   Contabo’s S3 URL

To note, **installing Rclone via your distro’s package manager can cause the download/upload speed to suffer.** 

#### **How do I configure rclone with Contabo’s Object Storage?**

1.  Run rclone config.
2.  Specify n for n) New remote.
3.  Specify a name, e.g. eu2.
4.  Specify 5 for Amazon S3 Compliant Storage Providers including AWS, Alibaba, ArvanCloud, Ceph, China Mobile, Cloudflare, GCS, DigitalOcean, Dreamhost, Huawei OBS, IBM COS, IDrive e2, IONOS Cloud, Liara, Lyve Cloud, Minio, Netease, Petabox, RackCorp, Scaleway, SeaweedFS, StackPath, Storj, Tencent COS, Qiniu and Wasabi.
5.  Specify 4 for Ceph Object Storage.
6.  Specify 1 for Enter AWS credentials in the next step.
7.  Specify your access\_key, e.g. 82046e8110804a43bf29c1ae426a724d for access\_key\_id.
8.  Specify your secret\_key, e.g. 82e69bd7a52076c527154297a76c2233 for secret\_access\_key.
9.  Press return to skip region.
10.  Specify the S3 URL, e.g. https://eu2.contabostorage.com for endpoint.
11.  Press return to skip location\_constraint.
12.  Press return to skip acl.
13.  Press return to skip server\_side\_encryption.
14.  Press return to skip sse\_kms\_key\_id.
15.  Specify n for Edit advanced config.
16.  Specify y for Yes this is OK (default).
17.  Optionally specify q to quit the configuration mode.

#### **How do I mount using rclone?**

Please note that that a mounted Object Storage bucket doesn’t always behave like a normal POSIX file system. Depending on how your application is accessing files, you may encounter issues like:

*   no/wrong file system usage statistics
*   sub-par performance
*   unexpected unmounts
*   duplicate writes leading to hidden objects that occupy space but are invisible to S3 API requests

Below you can find an example to mount a bucket to /mnt with recommend flags for optimal performance:

rclone mount eu2:bucketname /mnt --vfs-cache-mode full --vfs-fast-fingerprint --no-modtime

To learn more about available mount options, check the [rclone mount documentation](https://rclone.org/commands/rclone_mount/)
. 

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000305592-what-is-the-rclone-tool-)

# Why Do I Need to Verify My Purchase?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000348466-why-do-i-need-to-verify-my-purchase/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Why do I need to verify my purchase?**

We are obligated by law to perform customer data verification for each customer.

Without your response to the verification request email there is a risk that we can’t set up the products you have ordered. 

#### **How do I verify my purchase?** 

If you received a verification request from us, **you will need to follow the instructions in the email.** You will likely be asked to provide the following information:

*   First Name
*   Last Name
*   Address
*   City
*   ZIP Code
*   Country
*   Telephone Number
*   A scan of your passport, driving license, or national identity card
*   A scan of your telephone, electricity, or any other utility bill as long as it contains your name and your address

If possible, please send all scans as an attachment to your email and keep in mind that we can open only jpg, jpeg, png, gif, pdf, log, or txt files.

**To get your purchase verified as quickly as possible, please respond directly to the email requesting additional verification with the requested information.**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000348466-why-do-i-need-to-verify-my-purchase-)

# Can You Provide More Information On Contabo's IPFS Node Image?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000283084-can-you-provide-more-information-on-contabo-s-ipfs-node-image/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Can you provide more information on Contabo’s IPFS Node image?  
**

Contabo’s IPFS Node image is based on the latest Kubo Client Docker image ([https://github.com/ipfs/kubo](https://github.com/ipfs/kubo)
).

#### **How do I switch to the IPFS user?**

For accessing ipfs command you should switch to ipfs user.

sudo -u ipfs -i

#### **How do I retrieve the IPFS version?**

You can run the following command:

ipfs version

In turn you should get the following output:

ipfs version 0.22.0

#### **How do I access different IPFS file paths?**

You can find the different file paths below:

Staging folder: /var/lib/ipfs/staging/

Data folder: /var/lib/ipfs/data/

#### **How do I run docker commands?**

You can run the following command to retrieve docker containers:

docker ps

You can run the following command to read container logs:

docker logs ipfs\_host

**You can find the Kubo [documentation here](https://github.com/ipfs/kubo)
 which you may find useful.  
**

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000283084-can-you-provide-more-information-on-contabo-s-ipfs-node-image-)

# How to View Invoices and Payment History at Contabo

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/view-invoices-payment-history-contabo/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![YouTube thumbnail for a video about invoice and payment history.](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Invoice-and-Payment-History.png "Invoice and Payment History")

If you’ve ever wondered **how to view invoices and payment history at Contabo**, this guide will show you exactly where to look and how to manage your billing details effortlessly. When you’re busy managing websites or developing applications, the last thing you want is billing confusion that could lead to service interruptions. Fortunately, finding your complete payment history using the **Contabo Customer Control Panel (CCP)** is straightforward once you know where to look. This guide shows you exactly how to access and understand your billing information, so you can get back to what you do best. 

Prefer video? Watch the full tutorial here:

Why Reviewing Your Payment History Matters 
-------------------------------------------

Your **payment history** is more than just a log of transactions it’s a tool that helps you: 

*   Understand your recurring charges 

*   Track when payments were made (or missed) 

*   Manage your cash flow 

*   Plan for upcoming billing cycles

At Contabo, we aim to make this process as straightforward as possible, so you can focus on what matters whether that’s hosting websites, running cloud services, or deploying enterprise apps. 

Step-by-Step: How to Access Your Invoices and Payment History 
--------------------------------------------------------------

**1\. Log into the Customer Control Panel (CCP)** 

Head over to [contabo.com](https://contabo.com/)
 and log in using your customer credentials. Once you’re in, you’ll land on your **dashboard** your central hub for managing services, billing, and support. 

**2\. Navigate to the Billing Section** 

In the left-hand menu, click on **Account**, then select **Billing & Payment**. This will take you directly to your **payment history**. 

What You’ll See in the Payment History View 
--------------------------------------------

Your payment history provides a clean and organized overview of: 

*   **All past and current bookings** 

*   **Payments made** 

*   **Credit notes** 

*   **Recurring charges** 

By default, the system displays your transactions from the **last month**, but you can adjust the date range to view a longer or more specific timeframe. 

**Here’s what each item means:** 

*   **Payments**: Transactions where you’ve successfully paid for services. 

*   **Recurring Bookings**: Automatic charges for ongoing services. 

*   **Credit Notes**: Refunds or adjustments applied to your account. 

This view gives you a full picture of your financial activity with Contabo, all in one place. 

How Invoices Are Generated 
---------------------------

Your invoices are automatically generated at the **start of each month**. Each invoice includes **all bookings made since the previous billing cycle**, and you can download or view them directly from your billing section. 

**Key points to keep in mind:** 

*   Invoices may have **multiple pages**, depending on the number of bookings. 

*   If you’ve added an **invoice contact**, they’ll receive a copy via email automatically. 

*   Invoices are formatted clearly to help you understand what services you’re paying for. 

Managing Your Finances with Confidence 
---------------------------------------

With easy access to your invoice and payment data, you can: 

*   Stay on top of due payments 

*   Keep accurate records for accounting 

*   Monitor for any unexpected charges 

*   Make informed decisions about scaling your services 

Contabo’s transparent billing system is built to make your life easier, so you always know where your money is going. 

Why It Matters for Hosting Users 
---------------------------------

Whether you’re hosting a personal blog, managing client projects, or running a complex virtual infrastructure, staying financially organized is just as important as uptime and performance. 

Our Customer Control Pane l gives you **one place for everything** from managing your servers to staying on top of payments. 

Final Thoughts 
---------------

Clear financial records aren’t just about good bookkeeping, they’re your assurance that the digital infrastructure you depend on stays online without surprises. At Contabo, we know that great hosting isn’t just about hardware, it’s also about giving you **simple, clear tools** to manage your services. With the Billing & Payment section in your Control Panel, everything you need is right at your fingertips. 

Have any questions? Our support team is always happy to help. Until then, happy hosting

# How Do I Setup a SSH Connection?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000271398-how-do-i-setup-a-ssh-connection/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Why should I setup a SSH connection?**

SSH is a more secure way to connect to your server and prevents the connection from exposing your server’s ports to the Internet.

How to set up an SSH connection depends on the operating systems (OS) used on your local computer and the Contabo server. Below you will find instructions on how to set up an SSH connection depending on the OS used on your local computer and Contabo server.

#### **Table of Contents  
**

*   [How do I setup a SSH connection to a Windows Server Using a Windows Local Computer?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000271398-how-do-i-setup-a-ssh-connection/#How-do-I-setup-a-SSH-connection-to-a-Windows-Server-Using-a-Windows-Local-Computer)
    
*   [How do I setup a SSH connection on a Linux server?](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000271398-how-do-i-setup-a-ssh-connection/#How-do-I-setup-a-SSH-connection-on-a-Linux-server)
    

#### **How do I setup a SSH connection to a Windows Server Using a Windows Local Computer?**

1.  If your local computer runs on Windows, you will need to use PuTTY in combination with PuTTYgen to setup an SSH connection on your server.
    
    Install the PuTTY on your machine and then run PuTTYgen with the following steps.
    
    Go to **Windows -> Start Menu -> All Programs -> PuTTY -> PuTTYgen**
    
2.  The PuTTY key generator dialog box will appear on your screen:
    
    ![Generation ssh keys](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103082480909/original/J2j-MTTG0K301vmrdE6xC4ge-TDgCTTE0A.png?1706179409)
    
    Set a unique “**Key Comment**” to be able to identify the key later.
    
3.  Now you need to add a unique key passphrase to the “**Key passphrase**” and “**Confirm passphrase**” fields.
    
    It is important that you remember the passphrase because it will be used to load the private key (it is similar to a password).
    
4.  Click on the “**Save Public Key**” and “**Save Private Key**” buttons to save your public and private keys to a secure location.
5.  You will see the text starting with ssh-RSA in the Public key for pasting into OpenSSH authorized keys file field which is located at the top of the window.
    
    Copy all of this text to your clipboard using ctrl+c, as you will need to upload the public key to your server.
    
6.  Connect to your server via SSH.
7.  Check if the directory **~/ssh** is already present. If this is not the case, create it by executing the following command:
    
    `mkdir ~/.ssh`
    
8.  Open a text editor like vi to open the text file with:
    
    `vi ~/.ssh/authorized_keys`
    
9.  Paste the public key which you have copied to your clipboard into the file, then save the file.
    
    Now, you can use newly created SSH keys to log in with PuTTY. To set-up your keys in Putty, go to the settings and continue to **Connection > SSH > Auth.** Notice the last field on the bottom “Private key file for authentication”:
    
    ![load your key to PuTTY](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103082480910/original/KRzexA17kaSuCuwzF75Krak_mzBzOsAg0w.png?1706179409)
    
10.  Note that in case you’re about to reinstall your operating system using the [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
    , you can easily setup the SSH key before installation.    

#### **How do I setup a SSH connection on a Linux server?**

Linux servers are password-protected by default.

This type of authentication is not ideal from a security standpoint, and we recommend using SSH key authentication instead which you can setup with the following steps:

1.  Start by opening the command line on your personal computer.
2.  At this stage, you’re not connected to your server. Run this command:
    
    `ssh-keygen -t rsa`
    
3.  Depending on your version of Linux, your system will either automatically create a new file or prompt you for a name.
    
    Linux will ask you to enter an optional pass phrase for added security. We recommend that you use this option.
    
    Linux will now generate a random picture that looks like this:
    
    ![SSH key generation](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103082482816/original/BhyWcnj8aPXlMvq_mzwkjsOtjP3uyPVLZg.png?1706179625)
    
4.  Notice that a pair of keys was generated:
    
    Your private key has been saved in **<FILE\_NAME>**  
    Your public key has been saved in <**FILE\_NAME>.pub**.
    
5.  Your public key needs to be uploaded to your server while the private key stays on your computer.
    
    You can upload the same public key on multiple servers. Let’s move the public key to your server with the following command:
    
    `ssh-copy-id<YOUR_USERNAME>@<YOUR_IP>`
    
6.  In case you have already changed your port number use the option -p<YOUR\_PORT\_NUMBER> as shown in the below example command:
    
    `ssh-copy-id -p<YOUR_PORT_NUMBER><YOUR_USERNAME>@<YOUR_IP>`
    

Now you can connect to your server in a much safer way.

Note that in case you’re about to reinstall your operating system using [Customer Control Panel](https://my.contabo.com/)
, you can easily set-up the SSH key before installation with the following option during installation:

![ssh-public key installation](https://s3-eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/euc-cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/103082482812/original/Topi-ga8aVuVoG1sNFcVGTSqzYVSs8zWxQ.png?1706179625)

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000271398-how-do-i-setup-a-ssh-connection-)

# Welcome to Hub Europe – New Data Center for All Your Cloud Needs  | Contabo Blog

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/welcome-to-hub-europe-new-data-center-for-all-your-cloud-needs/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/image001.jpg)

The wait is over! We’re thrilled to share that Building 1 of our brand-new data center is up and running. Here’s an inside look at all the features that make Hub Europe the most reliable data center in the Contabo family. 

Hub Europe: **Home for 50,000 Servers** 
----------------------------------------

Hub Europe Data Center Park covers 25,600 square meters, which is roughly 4 football pitches.  The data center park will include up to 6 buildings with a total IT load of 9.6 MW. This means we can host up to 50,000 physical servers here. It was designed with N+1 redundancy to reach yearly availability of minimum 99.982% required for Tier 3 data centers. 

![Hub Europe](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/image-1.png)

Each building is independently connected to power grid and internet, and has a separate cooling system and secondary power system including UPS and diesel generator. Servers are stored in 2 server rooms, with each room holding ten rows of server racks. 

**Centrally Located with Premium Connectivity in Mind** 
--------------------------------------------------------

Hub Europe sits in a prime location, chosen to provide great connectivity across Europe and worldwide. It’s equipped with two fiber optic lines leading to the North and to the South into two different internet exchange points. We also contracted two different Tier 1 uplink providers with each providing two redundant 100 Gigabit lines. These lines will be extended as the traffic increases. 

This setup guarantees continuous internet connectivity, even if one physical uplink goes down or if one uplink provider experiences issues, as well as excellent latency across Europe. You can see below an outline of ping times from the new data center to key locations worldwide.

![](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/image005.jpg)

**Experienced Team on Site** 
-----------------------------

We have put together a team of seasoned professionals led by a manager with 20+ years of experience in data center operations. The team members have been working together for over a decade and have offices on-site, so as to be ready to react 24/7. 

**Redundant Power Connection**
------------------------------

The data center park is located **200 meters away from a power transformer station**. This ensures a stable and reliable power supply from the public grid. Two separate power lines have been built to provide redundancies. 

We follow the same level of redundancy all the way from high-voltage transformers down to the server racks. In the unlikely event that both power lines are out, **uninterruptible power supply (UPS)** and diesel generator ensure alternative power supply for days.  

Before the launch we performed extensive load bank testing. We used load banks to generate artificial IT load to ensure that the Building 1 works seamlessly at the designed capacity of 1.6 MW. 

![Hub Europe ](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/image.png)

**Resilient Cooling System** 
-----------------------------

The cooling system is based on **direct-free-cooling**. In this system the cool air flows through the raised floor and the cold aisles into the racks while the hot air generated by the servers is exhausted into the hot aisles. Then the hot air moves up and then gets pushed outside the building. The roof slope allows for extra space for the hot air away from the servers. 

This method minimizes the power needed for mechanical ventilation and allows for outside air to be used for cooling for 6-8 months each year. If the outside temperatures aren’t low enough to ensure proper cooling, **36 compressors** step in to maintain a safe temperature for the servers. Overall, this reduces energy usage by up to 60% compared to a setup where mechanical ventilation and cooling needs to be used on a constant basis.  

![Hub Europe](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/image.jpeg)

There are also several additional benefits to this system, as there are fewer moving parts, lower operational and maintenance costs. This allows us to keep great prices even when the other costs are increasing. 

As a consequence, Hub Europe has a design Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of 1.168, which makes it one of the most energy-efficient data centers park in Europe. PUE is a global standard that shows how much electricity is needed to run a certain amount of IT load. In our case this means that to run servers consuming 1 kW we need 1.168 kW, with the remaining 0.1168 kW going predominantly into cooling. 

**100% Green Energy**
---------------------

![A building with construction equipment  ](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

We are not only optimizing the amount of power we need. We also took care of where it comes from. Hub Europe is powered by **100% carbon free energy**. What’s more; we’ve installed **photovoltaic panels** on each building, amounting to a total of more than 300 kWp (kilowatts peak rating). This further reduces our environmental impact and makes it possible to safeguard our low prices for our customers. We are in the process of obtaining ISO 50001 certification proving the energy efficiency of this data center park. 

**Hub Europe: Keeping Your Instances Safe**  
---------------------------------------------

Safety is our top priority, so we did our best to protect your data and our infrastructure against various risks. Before starting construction, we performed an extensive study on potential natural disasters risk in the area. Based on the results, we put in place several preventative measures. 

Our data center is equipped with **fire alarm** and nitrogen **fire extinguishers** that activate immediately if needed. **Fireproof doors** are installed to prevent fire from spreading between server rooms. 

We have raised the floor by 1.2 m and installed a high-performance pump to ensure that the entire site can withstand a 300-year flood. Flood gates are also available on site for swift installation, should it be necessary. This is on top of the regular flood prevention measures planned by the local government. 

**Lightning protection** is also done according to international standards. 

Several state-of-art physical security measures are in place; however, we shall keep those to ourselves for, well… security reasons :).  

**What’s Next for Hub Europe?** 
--------------------------------

Building 1 is already fully operational and already serving customers with over 20,000 instances running as of time of writing. Buildings 2 and 3 are planned to come online in 2025 (Q1 and Q3 respectively). 

This is a new and exciting chapter for Contabo. With Hub Europe our customers will experience more stable performance and better redundancy for the same wallet-friendly price. Follow us on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/contabo-gmbh/)
, [Twitter](https://x.com/ContaboCom)
 and [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/ContaboCom)
 for more updates as we move forward with this project.

# Wird die Migration von Nürnberg in den Hub Europe Center Park zu Ausfallzeiten führen, und wenn ja, für wie lange?

[Zum Inhalt springen](https://contabo.com/blog/de/kb/103000311127-wird-die-migration-von-nuernberg-in-den-hub-europe-center-park-zu-ausfallzeiten-fuehren-und-wenn-ja-f/#content "Zum Inhalt springen")

Hosten Sie Ihren eigenen KI-Agenten mit [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/de/openclaw-hosting/)
 - kostenlose 1-Klick-Installation!

#### **Kommt es durch die Migration zu Ausfallzeiten, und wenn ja, für wie lange?**

Je nachdem, welchen Servertyp Sie bei Contabo haben, kann es zu Ausfallzeiten kommen.

**Wenn Sie einen Dedicated Server haben wird es zu Ausfallzeiten kommen, die jedoch über Nacht geplant werden und maximal 14 Stunden andauern.** Wir benachrichtigen Sie 3 Wochen im Voraus, senden Ihnen kurz vor der Migration eine Erinnerung und bestätigen Ihnen den Abschluss des Prozesses.

**Wenn Sie einen VPS haben, wird es nur eine sehr geringe Ausfallzeit geben.** VPS-Migrationen werden online durchgeführt. Wenn eine Ausfallzeit bemerkbar ist, dauert sie weniger als 1 Minute.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/de/support/solutions/articles/103000311127-wird-die-migration-von-n%C3%BCrnberg-in-den-hub-europe-center-park-zu-ausfallzeiten-f%C3%BChren-und-wenn-ja-f)

# Why is My IP Address In the Wrong Country?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000280434-why-is-my-ip-address-in-the-wrong-country/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

#### **Why is My IP Address In the Wrong Country?**

At Contabo new IPv4 addresses are often obtained from 3rd parties and then assigned to new countries.

Because of this, some Contabo IP addresses were previously assigned to different countries and are still listed as assigned to the country they were previously assigned to by the 3rd party they were obtained from. **It can take a while for these 3rd parties to update this information on their site, causing the IP address to appear from the wrong country.**

**Rest assured that we use all of these IP addresses only in the location where your server is located.** A traceroute from your local device to the IP address of your server should show that the IP address is indeed assigned to the correct country.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000280434-why-is-my-ip-address-in-the-wrong-country-)

# What is the Auto Backup Add-On?

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/kb/103000331729-what-is-the-auto-backup-add-on/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

The Auto Backup Add-On is an optional extension to the functionality of Contabo’s Virtual Private Servers (VPS)  that creates automatically daily backups of your VPS data and stores them off-server for up to 10 days.  

The data is copied to a separate Contabo storage system which means data loss on the host-system of the VPS does not affect backed up data.

You can easily restore any available backup to your VPS anytime by following our [guide here](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000331776--draft-do-not-use-how-can-i-restore-my-vps-to-the-latest-auto-backup-)
.

[Details…](https://help.contabo.com/en/support/solutions/articles/103000331729-what-is-the-auto-backup-add-on-)

# How to install Java on Ubuntu using Apt-Get | Contabo Blog

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/how-to-install-java-on-ubuntu-using-apt-get/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![How To Install Java on Ubuntu using Apt-Get](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/blog-head_how2-install-Java-on-Ubuntu-with-Apt-Get1.jpg)

[Java](https://www.java.com/en/)
 is a widely used programming language that has become an essential tool for developing and running applications on various platforms. If you are a developer or a system administrator using Ubuntu 20.04, you may need to install Java on your machine to run Java-based applications or develop Java programs. Fortunately, Ubuntu 20.04 provides an easy and convenient way to install Java using the Apt-Get package manager. In this article, we will walk you through the step-by-step process of installing Java on Ubuntu 20.04 using Apt-Get, so you can get started with your Java projects right away. The process **is the same** for **Ubuntu 18.04** and **Ubuntu 22.04**. 

Installation of the Default JRE/JDK
-----------------------------------

If you are planning to use the current version of Java, which at the time of this writing is Openjdk 11.0.18 you can use the following two commands to either install the default JRE which stands for Java Runtime Envoirement or the JDK which stands for Java Developement Kit and is usually needed to compile Java applications. 

For the default JRE the command is: 

    apt-get install default-jre

And for the default JDK the command is: 

    apt-get install default-jdk

_Pro-Tip:_ The JDK always contains the JRE, so you do nothing wrong when always going with the JDK apart from its larger file size. 

Installing other Java Versions
------------------------------

It may happen that you need older versions of Java for your project. Because the commands shown above only install the latest version of either the JDK or the JRE, you need to specifically install the older versions. But no worries! We got you covered! 

### Installing Java 8 (JDK & JRE)

To install Java 8 JDK use this command: 

    apt-get install openjdk-8-jdk –y

And to install Java 8 JRE use this command: 

    apt-get install openjdk-8-jre –y

### Installing Java 16 (JDK & JRE) 

Installing the Java 16 JDK or JRE are again very easy and can be done with one single command. 

For Java 16 JDK this command would be: 

    apt-get install openjdk-16-jdk –y 

And for Java 16 JRE it would be: 

    apt-get install openjdk-16-jre –y 

### Installing Java 17 (JDK & JRE) 

Ok… now it’s getting repetitive but you may have guessed it: Java 17 is installed in the same way, but with “17” as the version number.  

So to install Java 17 JDK use this command: 

    apt-get install openjdk-17-jdk –y 

And for Java 17 JRE use this command: 

    apt-get install openjdk-17-jre –y 

Switching between different Java Versions 
------------------------------------------

It is possible to have several Java versions installed at the same time, but only one Java version is selected or active at a time. This is especially important when you want to set up your own Minecraft Server and you are often switching between Minecraft versions. If you want to learn more about how to run your own Minecraft Server, read our beginner-friendly Minecraft-Server guide [here](https://contabo.com/blog/minecraft-server-setup-ultimate-guide/)
. 

You can find out which version is currently active with this command: 

    java -version

The output will look simillar to this: 

![How To Install Java on Ubuntu using Apt-Get](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/image.png)

As you can see, although I’ve installed multiple versions of Java, Java 17 is currently active. To switch between the active versions use this command: 

    sudo update-alternatives --config java 

Then this table will show up: 

![How To Install Java on Ubuntu using Apt-Get](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/image-1.png)

If you want to switch to a different version of java, just type in the “Selection”-Number of the corresponding version of Java (e.g. “4” for Java 8) and hit enter. 

How to set the “JAVA\_HOME” environment Variable
------------------------------------------------

The JAVA\_HOME variable is an environment variable that points to the root directory of the Java installation on your system. It is used by Java-based applications to locate the Java installation and execute Java commands. 

Setting the JAVA\_HOME variable is important for development environments or when running applications that require a specific Java version. For example, if you have multiple Java versions installed on your system, setting the JAVA\_HOME variable to the desired version can ensure that the correct Java version is used when running an application. 

To set the JAVA\_HOME variable, follow these steps: 

Run this command to get an overview of every installed version and their path: 

    sudo update-alternatives --config java 

This window will pop up again: 

![How To Install Java on Ubuntu using Apt-Get](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/image-2.png)

Here you can see every installed Java version and the corresponding path, e.g. the Path for the Java 17 JDK is “/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64/bin/java” 

If you don’t want to change anything here and just wanted to find the path to copy it, press **\[Enter\]** to close the menu again. 

Now open the file, which will contain the variable with this command: 

    sudo nano /etc/environment 

And add the following line: 

    JAVA_HOME="YOUR_PATH" 

(Of course, replace “YOUR PATH” with the actual path of the java version) 

Save the file with **\[CTRL\]+O** and close the editor with **\[CTRL\]+X**. 

For the changes to take effect, reload the config with this command: 

    source /etc/environment 

To verify that everything worked, use this command to test it: 

    echo $JAVA_HOME 

It should return the path you’ve just entered. 

**Congrats!** Now you know how to install different versions of Java and how to switch between them.

# Plesk Hosting Control Panel – Guide from Contabo

[Skip to content](https://contabo.com/blog/plesk-hosting-control-panel/#content "Skip to content")

Host Your Own AI Agent with [OpenClaw](https://contabo.com/en/openclaw-hosting/)
 - Free 1-Click Setup!

![Plesk control panel dashboard for managing websites, apps, and servers with Contabo hosting](https://contabo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/What-is-Plesk.png "Plesk Hosting Control Panel – Simplify Server Management with Contabo")

Managing servers and websites can sometimes feel like you’re juggling too many tools at once. That’s where **Plesk Hosting Control Panel** comes in. If you’ve been in the hosting world for a while, you’ve probably heard the name. But what exactly is Plesk, and should you be using it? 

In this post, we’ll walk you through what Plesk does, who it’s for, and how it compares to other control panels like cPanel. Whether you’re running a single blog or managing a dozen client websites, this guide will help you figure out if Plesk is the right fit. 

What Exactly Does Plesk Do?
---------------------------

Think of Plesk as a control center for your web hosting. It takes care of the heavy lifting behind the scenes, so you don’t need deep technical knowledge to run your server. 

With Plesk, you can: 

*   **Manage multiple websites and domains** from a single, clean interface 

*   **Install apps like WordPress** with just one click 

*   **Set up email accounts and databases** without touching the command line 

*   **Protect your site with built-in tools** like SSL, a firewall, and fail2ban 

*   **Improve performance** using caching, CDN support, and automatic updates 

One of the great things about Plesk is that it works across both **Linux and Windows servers**. It also supports popular web servers like Apache, NGINX, and LiteSpeed, giving you the flexibility to match your preferred setup. 

So, whether you’re launching a blog, managing client projects, or offering hosting services, Plesk offers a convenient and powerful way to stay in control. 

Do You Really Need Plesk? 
--------------------------

The answer depends on how you like to manage your hosting environment. Here’s a quick breakdown to help you decide. 

**You’ll Probably Want Plesk If:** 

*   You handle multiple websites and want a centralized, user-friendly interface 

*   You prefer tools that make setup quick – like one-click WordPress installs 

*   You’re running a hosting business or reselling hosting space to clients 

If this sounds like you, Plesk can save you hours of technical setup and reduce the chance of making mistakes with manual configurations. 

**You Might Not Need Plesk If:** 

*   You’re comfortable working directly in Linux using the command line 

*   You’re only running a single, simple site and don’t mind setting things up manually 

*   You already use and prefer another control panel like **cPanel** or **DirectAdmin** 

For advanced users who enjoy full control over every setting, or those already set in their ways, Plesk may feel unnecessary. But for most users who want something that “just works,” Plesk is a helpful companion. 

Plesk vs. cPanel: What’s the Difference? 
-----------------------------------------

If you’re comparing control panels, **Plesk and cPanel** are likely your top two contenders. 

Here’s how they compare: 

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Feature** | **Plesk** | **cPanel** |
| **Operating System Support** | Linux & Windows | Linux only |
| **User Interface** | Modern and cleaner design | Traditional but powerful |
| **Multi-Website Management** | Strong support | Also strong, but more CLI-focused |
| **Ease of Use for Beginners** | Very user-friendly | Slightly more technical |

Both platforms are strong and reliable. Plesk tends to be favored by those who want a modern interface and Windows compatibility, while cPanel is often chosen for its long-standing track record in Linux environments. 

At **Contabo**, we offer **Plesk as an optional Add-On**, making it simple to include during your server setup. It’s a great way to streamline your hosting experience right from the start. 

So, Is Plesk Right for You? 
----------------------------

If you’re looking for a control panel that simplifies server management, offers built-in security tools, and makes it easy to launch apps with just a click, **Plesk is a great choice**. 

It’s particularly useful for: 

*   Web developers managing client sites 

*   Hosting resellers who need a polished, scalable solution 

*   Website owners who want more control without the technical stress 

And the best part? You can get **Plesk directly through Contabo**, so setup is seamless, and you’re up and running in no time. 

Final Thoughts 
---------------

Server and website management doesn’t have to be complicated. Plesk removes much of the technical guesswork and replaces it with a streamlined, intuitive platform that works the way you do. 

If you’re curious to try it out, consider adding Plesk when you set up your next server at Contabo. It’s easy, powerful, and designed to grow with your needs.
